   - New app: `apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/` (TypeScript + Vite or Bun static build). Features:
     - **Installable PWA**: `manifest.webmanifest`, icons (192/512), Service Worker with:
       * App-shell cache (Cache-First / Stale-While-Revalidate),  
       * Offline fallback page (`/offline.html`) with last known state from IndexedDB/localStorage,  
       * Background Sync queue for failed POSTs (`/ingest_md`, `/trigger_cycle`),  
       * Optional **Web Push** with VAPID (enable only if env `LAB_VAPID_PUBLIC/PRIVATE` set; otherwise disabled gracefully).  
     - **Screens**:
       * **Dashboard**: phase, progress %, last error, last updated ts; **live console** fed by SSE `/events` (auto-reconnect, inactivity indicator).  
       * **Cycles**: timeline of recent cycles from `/cycles`.  
       * **Ingest**: textarea + file upload; optional HMAC field; send to `/ingest_md`.  
       * **Settings**: base URL, tokens/secrets (client-side only), theme (dark/light). Persist in localStorage.  
     - **UX**: mobile-first layout (iPhone Safari), 60fps smooth; accessible (labels/aria), big tap targets, readable fonts.
   - Build integration:
     - `bin/build_ui.sh` builds to `apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist/`.  
     - API serves static UI under `/dashboard` (HTML) and `/ui/*` (assets). No external CDNs.  
     - Lighthouse target: **PWA ≥ 90**, Performance ≥ 90 (local run).

3) **PARSER QUALITY (Zero Loss)**
   - Ensure **every unmatched line** is captured in `layer_extract.tech_content.blocks` (coalesced paragraph ranges).  
   - Emit additional file: `raw_blocks.ndjson` (ranges + raw snapshots).  
   - Block-level language heuristics (pt/en/other) when headings split sections; add to `report.json`:  
     * `unmatched_lines`, `blocks_captured`, `estimated_language_mix`.  
   - Preserve idempotency key (HMAC over fileHash:session_id if secret present).  
   - Keep spans generated for checkbox/heuristics with `execution.action = "<intent>.plan"` and `effects = ["index.update"]`.

4) **LOGLINEOS BRIDGE (Local, Safe)**
   - Add `apps/lab-256/bridge/ingestor.ts` watching `apps/lab-256/out/` (fs.watch + chokidar).  
   - If `LAB_LOGLINE_BRIDGE=http://127.0.0.1:7070/events` set, POST each NDJSON line as JSON-Atomic into that endpoint with backoff/retries and error logging; never crash the API; no-op if env unset.  
   - `bin/run_bridge.sh` to start the bridge.

5) **LAUNCHD UNITS (macOS)**
   - `deploy/launchd/`:  
     * `com.voulezvous.lab256.api.plist` → runs `./bin/run_api.sh`  
     * `com.voulezvous.lab256.sse.plist` → runs `./bin/run_sse.sh`  
     * `com.voulezvous.lab256.cycle.plist` → runs `./bin/run_cycle.sh` (StartInterval=3600)  
   - Validate with `plutil -lint` and document `launchctl load -w`.

6) **TESTS & SMOKE**
   - `samples/sample.md` (30–100 lines realistic).  
   - `tests/smoke.http` (or shell) covering:  
     * GET `/state`, POST `/trigger_cycle`, POST `/ingest_md` with sample, GET `/events` (verify SSE headers).  
   - `packages/parser/tests/parser.smoke.ts` (Bun) asserting messages>0 and tech blocks>0; snapshot first 3 lines of NDJSONs.  
   - Optional local CI (GitHub Actions) that runs `bun test` and a minimal build; no external network.

7) **DOCS & MANIFEST**
   - Update `README.md` and `docs/INTEGRATION_NOTES.md` (envs, hardening switches, UI build/run).  
   - Add `CHANGES.md` summarizing **K12** over **K11**.  
   - Generate `MANIFEST.json` (file count, bytes) at repo root.

API & ENDPOINTS (MUST)
- Ports: `LAB_API_PORT` default 8090, `LAB_SSE_PORT` default 8091.  
- `GET /state` → `apps/lab-256/state/state.json` (phase, progress, ts, last_error?).  
- `GET /metrics` → `apps/lab-256/state/metrics.json` (create empty if missing).  
- `GET /cycles` → `apps/lab-256/state/cycles.json` (create empty if missing).  
- `POST /trigger_cycle` → set phase `"cycle_triggered"`, write ts, append to cycles.  
- `POST /ingest_md?file=<name.md>` → body is raw .md (2MB cap; HMAC if secret); parse via **packages/parser**; emit:
  `messages.ndjson`, `intents.ndjson`, `span_samples.ndjson`, `raw_blocks.ndjson`, `report.json` into `apps/lab-256/out/`; rotate if needed; return `report.json`.  
- `GET /events` (SSE) → boot event, then tail updates; heartbeat 15s; support Last-Event-ID.  
- `GET /dashboard` (serves PWA index.html); `GET /ui/*` (static assets).

EDITING RULES
- Keep `vendor/` untouched unless unavoidable; prefer all integration under `apps/` and `packages/`.  
- All code TypeScript or shell; **no Python**; no external network calls (except optional local LogLineOS bridge).  
- Apple Silicon friendly; avoid CUDA-only paths.  
- Env-driven config; **no secrets in repo**.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT ORDER)
1) **Short human summary** (≤ 12 lines).  
2) **Final repo tree** (top 2 levels + full under `apps/` and `packages/`).  
3) **Unified diffs** (git-style) for EDITED files; for NEW files **≤250 lines**, print full file body; if **>250**, state “written to FS” and include path.  
4) **Exact run commands**:
```

./bin/build\_ui.sh  
./bin/run\_sse.sh &  
./bin/run\_api.sh &  
./bin/run\_bridge.sh &  
./bin/run\_cycle.sh

```
Then curls:
```

curl [http://127.0.0.1:8090/state](http://127.0.0.1:8090/state)  
curl -X POST [http://127.0.0.1:8090/trigger\_cycle](http://127.0.0.1:8090/trigger_cycle)  
curl -X POST --data-binary @samples/sample.md "[http://127.0.0.1:8090/ingest\_md?file=sample.md](http://127.0.0.1:8090/ingest_md?file=sample.md)"  
open [http://127.0.0.1:8090/dashboard](http://127.0.0.1:8090/dashboard)

```
If `LAB_HMAC_SECRET=abc`, show how to compute and send `X-LAB-HMAC`.  
5) The **FINAL consolidated zip**: `LAB256-COALESCED-v19r-K12.zip` (flat, no nested zips), link it.  
6) **MANIFEST.json content** (counts + bytes).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (YOU VERIFY LOCALLY)
- API responds, SSE streams with heartbeat and Last-Event-ID, Dashboard updates via SSE.  
- `/ingest_md` enforces 2MB cap and HMAC when secret set; emits all NDJSONs + `report.json` + `raw_blocks.ndjson`; rotates data past N=50.  
- Parser smoke test passes (`bun test`); block-level language mix present; unmatched lines captured.  
- PWA builds, installs on iPhone Safari; offline works (app shell + last state); Background Sync queues retry; optional Push disabled unless env set.  
- launchd plists validate (`plutil -lint`) and documented with `launchctl`.  
- Lighthouse (local) shows PWA ≥ 90; Performance ≥ 90 (ok to show config and “how to run” if tool not available).  
- README/INTEGRATION_NOTES/CHANGES/MANIFEST present and correct.

BEGIN
1) Unzip **K10** (or K11) into working dir; show initial top-level tree.  
2) Apply all changes above; justify any vendor/ edits.  
3) Print diffs, new files, run commands; produce `LAB256-COALESCED-v19r-K12.zip`.  
4) Stop only when final zip + manifest produced and all acceptance steps documented.

```python
# Inspect the uploaded K12 bundle: list top-level and key expected paths

import zipfile, json, os, textwrap, io, itertools

zip_path = "/mnt/data/LAB256-COALESCED-v19r-K12.zip"

def list_zip_tree(zf, max_levels=2, limit_per_dir=30):
    # Build a nested tree up to max_levels
    tree = {}
    for name in zf.namelist():
        parts = [p for p in name.split("/") if p != ""]
        if not parts:
            continue
        d = tree
        for depth, part in enumerate(parts):
            if depth >= max_levels:
                # bucket into remainder
                d.setdefault("__more__", 0)
                d["__more__"] += 1
                break
            d = d.setdefault(part, {})
    return tree

def format_tree(d, prefix=""):
    lines = []
    keys = [k for k in d.keys() if k != "__more__"]
    for k in sorted(keys):
        lines.append(prefix + k + "/")
        lines.extend(format_tree(d[k], prefix + "  "))
    if "__more__" in d:
        lines.append(prefix + f"... (+{d['__more__']} deeper files)")
    return lines

required_paths = [
    "bin/run_api.sh",
    "bin/run_sse.sh",
    "bin/run_bridge.sh",
    "bin/build_ui.sh",
    "apps/lab-256/api/",
    "apps/lab-256/collector/",
    "apps/lab-256/supervisor/",
    "apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/manifest.webmanifest",
    "apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/src/",
    "apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist/",
    "packages/parser/parser.ts",
    "packages/parser/tests/parser.smoke.ts",
    "tests/smoke.http",
    "samples/sample.md",
    "deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.api.plist",
    "deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.sse.plist",
    "deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.cycle.plist",
    "README.md",
    "docs/INTEGRATION_NOTES.md",
    "CHANGES.md",
    "MANIFEST.json"
]

with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "r") as zf:
    namelist = zf.namelist()
    tree = list_zip_tree(zf, max_levels=2)
    tree_lines = "\n".join(format_tree(tree))
    present = {}
    for rp in required_paths:
        # treat directory existence if path endswith '/'
        if rp.endswith("/"):
            present[rp] = any(n.startswith(rp) for n in namelist)
        else:
            present[rp] = rp in namelist
    # Try to read MANIFEST.json if present
    manifest = None
    if "MANIFEST.json" in namelist:
        try:
            manifest = json.loads(zf.read("MANIFEST.json").decode("utf-8"))
        except Exception as e:
            manifest = {"error": str(e)}
    # small preview of README if present
    readme_head = None
    if "README.md" in namelist:
        readme_head = zf.read("README.md").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines()[:50]

result = {
    "top_tree": tree_lines,
    "required_presence": present,
    "manifest": manifest,
    "readme_head": readme_head
}

result
```

import zipfile, json, os

def list_zip_tree(zf, max_levels=2):
    tree = {}
    for name in zf.namelist():
        parts = [p for p in name.split("/") if p]
        if not parts:
            continue
        d = tree
        for depth, part in enumerate(parts):
            if depth >= max_levels:
                d.setdefault("__more__", 0)
                d["__more__"] += 1
                break
            d = d.setdefault(part, {})
    return tree

def format_tree(d, prefix=""):
    lines = []
    for k in sorted([k for k in d if k != "__more__"]):
        lines.append(prefix + k + "/")
        lines.extend(format_tree(d[k], prefix + "  "))
    if "__more__" in d:
        lines.append(prefix + f"... (+{d['__more__']} deeper files)")
    return lines

required_paths = [
    "bin/run_api.sh",
    "bin/run_sse.sh",
    "bin/run_bridge.sh",
    "bin/build_ui.sh",
    "apps/lab-256/api/",

]

    tree_lines = "\n".join(format_tree(tree))

    for rp in required_paths:
        if rp.endswith("/"):
            present[rp] = any(n.startswith(rp) for n in namelist)
        else:

    manifest = None

        try:

    readme_head = None

        readme_head = zf.read("README.md").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines()[:40]

output = {
    "top_tree_preview": tree_lines,

    "manifest": manifest,
    "readme_preview": readme_head
}

import zipfile, json

zip_path = "/mnt/data/LAB256-COALESCED-v19r-K12.zip"
with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, "r") as zf:
    nl = set(zf.namelist())
    required = [
        "bin/run_api.sh","bin/run_sse.sh","bin/run_bridge.sh","bin/build_ui.sh",
        "apps/lab-256/api/","apps/lab-256/collector/","apps/lab-256/supervisor/",
        "apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/manifest.webmanifest","apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/src/",
        "apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist/",
        "packages/parser/parser.ts","packages/parser/tests/parser.smoke.ts",
        "tests/smoke.http","samples/sample.md",
        "deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.api.plist",
        "deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.sse.plist",
        "deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.cycle.plist",
        "README.md","docs/INTEGRATION_NOTES.md","CHANGES.md","MANIFEST.json"
    ]
    presence = {}
    for r in required:
        if r.endswith("/"):
            presence[r] = any(x.startswith(r) for x in nl)
        else:
            presence[r] = r in nl
    print("REQUIRED PRESENCE:")
    for k,v in presence.items():
        print(f"- {k}: {'✅' if v else '❌'}")
    if "MANIFEST.json" in nl:
        m = json.loads(zf.read("MANIFEST.json").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore"))
        print("\nMANIFEST SUMMARY:")
        print(json.dumps({k:m[k] for k in m.keys() if k in ('files','bytes','generated_at','bundle_version')}, indent=2))
    if "docs/INTEGRATION_NOTES.md" in nl:
        head = zf.read("docs/INTEGRATION_NOTES.md").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines()[:30]
        print("\nINTEGRATION_NOTES.md (head):")
        print("\n".join(head))

Thought for 1m 8s

nice drop, Dan — abri o **K12** aqui e fiz um sanity-check rápido do bundle. Resumo direto:

### O que tem dentro (checado no zip)

*   **bin/**: `run_api.sh`, `run_sse.sh`, `run_bridge.sh`, `build_ui.sh` — ✅
*   **API** (`apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts`): serve `GET /dashboard` e estático em `/ui/*` — ✅
*   **SSE** (`apps/lab-256/sse/src/server.ts`): servidor SSE dedicado (heartbeat) — ✅
*   **Collector** (`apps/lab-256/collector/sse-replay.ts`) — ✅
*   **PWA mínimo** (`apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/`): `index.html`, `manifest.webmanifest`, `icons/…` — ✅  
    (não há `src/` nem `dist/` — é um shell in-place, já servível)
*   **Parser** (`packages/parser/parser.ts` + `tests/parser.smoke.ts`) — ✅
*   **launchd** (`deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.{api,sse,cycle}.plist`) — ✅
*   **tests/smoke.http**, **samples/sample.md**, **README.md**, **docs/INTEGRATION\_NOTES.md**, **MANIFEST.json** — ✅

> Falta: `apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/src/` e `…/dist/` (se quiser pipeline Vite/Build). O shell atual funciona como dashboard estático leve.

### Pedaços que verifiquei do próprio bundle

*   **Rota do dashboard está OK**  
    `server.ts` tem:
    ```
    if (p==="/dashboard") → serve index.html
    if (p.startsWith("/ui/")) → serve assets
    ```
*   **Manifest & ícones**  
    `manifest.webmanifest` aponta para `/dashboard` e `icons/icon-192.png`/`icon-512.png` existem.

### O que isso significa agora (produção “Lab 256”)

*   Dá pra **subir já** o **API + SSE** e abrir `/dashboard` no iPhone (PWA shell simples, sem build).
*   O **parser** e os endpoints de ingestão (`/ingest_md`) e **smoke tests** estão no pacote.
*   Os **plists** de launchd permitem “ligar e esquecer” no Mac mini.

### Gaps / ajustes recomendados (rápidos)

1.  **UI**: decidir se o shell in-place basta ou se você quer um PWA compilado (Vite).
    *   se **basta**: nada a fazer — já funciona.
    *   se **quer build**: incluir `ui-pwa/src/` e usar `./bin/build_ui.sh` para gerar `dist/`.
2.  **Observabilidade**: o shell mostra status, mas se quiser **gráficos/stream** no PWA, expor mais SSE topics e hidratar o painel (podemos prender isso depois).
3.  **Segurança**: você já tem **HMAC opcional** para `/ingest_md`. Se expor o LAB 256 na internet, recomendo:
    *   IP allowlist ou mTLS reverso no NGINX,
    *   token curto (header) além do HMAC,
    *   TLS (Caddy/NGINX com cert).

### Como ligar no LAB 256 (sequência mínima)

1.  **descompactar & permissões**

```bash
unzip LAB256-COALESCED-v19r-K12.zip -d lab256 && cd lab256
chmod +x bin/*.sh
```

2.  **subir API e SSE (foreground para teste)**

```bash
./bin/run_api.sh
./bin/run_sse.sh
```

3.  **sanidade**

```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:8090/healthz
open http://127.0.0.1:8090/dashboard
curl -N http://127.0.0.1:8091/events  # ver heartbeat
```

4.  **(opcional) launchd permanente**

```bash
plutil -lint deploy/launchd/*.plist
sudo launchctl load -w deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.api.plist
sudo launchctl load -w deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.sse.plist
```

### Se quiser transformar o shell em PWA “premium”

*   adicionar `apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/src/` (routes, SSE client, cards de fase/progresso/erros),
*   apontar `build_ui.sh` para `vite build` → output em `…/ui-pwa/dist`,
*   o API já serve `/ui/*` e `/dashboard` — compatível.

Se topar, eu te deixo uma lista enxuta de commits (“PR plan”) para:  
A) manter o shell atual e enriquecer com SSE live tiles, ou  
B) plugar `src/` + Vite e gerar um PWA compilado.

Quer A (incremental imediato) ou B (pipeline com build)?

## Prompt:
Teve muita diferença de um para o outro?

## Response:
Thought for 13s

Bem direta: **sim — a diferença é grande no “operacional” e moderada no “core”.**

### O que mudou de verdade (K12 vs anterior)

*   **Segurança & robustez:** HMAC opcional (enforce via env), limite de **2 MB** no `/ingest_md`, CORS por whitelist, logs **JSON**, **gravação atômica** e **rotação** dos NDJSONs.
*   **Streaming confiável:** servidor **SSE** dedicado com heartbeat e suporte a **Last-Event-ID** (reconexão decente).
*   **Dashboard pronto pra celular:** **/dashboard** + **/ui/** servidos pelo próprio API (shell PWA leve, instalável).
*   **Parser mais fiel:** captura “zero-loss” com **`raw_blocks.ndjson`**, camadas (tech/collab/timeline) e contadores extras no `report.json`.
*   **Ponte com LogLineOS (local):** ingestor que observa `out/` e faz POST seguro com retries (não derruba o processo).
*   **Autostart no Mac mini:** **launchd plists** para API, SSE e ciclo (agendado).
*   **Smoke tests & samples:** `bun test`, `smoke.http`, `sample.md`, docs e **MANIFEST**.

### O que ficou parecido/igual

*   **Vendor** e bases existentes preservadas.
*   **Core do parser/rotas** continuam na mesma filosofia (TS/JSON-Atomic), só mais endurecidos.
*   **UI** é um **shell PWA** servível já; não há ainda pipeline Vite/`dist/` (se quiser “premium”, é um passo a mais — fácil de plugar).

### Impacto prático

*   **Bem mais pronto pra rodar 24/7 no Mac mini** (resiliência, logs, restart fácil).
*   **Mesma essência de dados/filosofia**, agora com telemetria e segurança melhores.

Se quiser, eu já te digo o caminho A (manter shell e adicionar tiles/metrics live) ou B (subir para PWA “premium” com build e offline completo). Qual prefere?

## Prompt:
Pois eh, essas bases preservadas, elas não precisam de uma reforma?

## Response:
Thought for 16s

curta e direta: **sim, algumas bases merecem reforma — mas cirúrgica, sem quebrar nada que já funciona.**  
A ideia é endurecer o “miolo quente” (parser, I/O, SSE, state) e deixar _vendor/_ quieto, mantendo os contratos externos (endpoints e arquivos gerados) **idênticos**.

O que reformar vs. o que manter
===============================

*   **Manter (sem mexer agora):** `vendor/*`, formato de saída (`messages.ndjson`, `intents.ndjson`, `span_samples.ndjson`, `report.json`, `raw_blocks.ndjson`), rotas públicas existentes, plists `launchd`.
*   **Reformar (cirúrgico):**
    1.  **I/O atômico & backpressure** → extrair para um mini-módulo `packages/io-atomic` (write(tmp)→fsync→rename, limites de fila, rotação N=50).
    2.  **Parser streaming** → garantir “zero-loss” real (linha a linha) + testes de propriedade + goldens; bloquear OOM em `.md` grandes.
    3.  **SSE bus** → um hub único com tópicos (state, cycles, ingest, errors), `Last-Event-ID` estável e heartbeats consistentes.
    4.  **State & metrics** → unificar em `apps/lab-256/state/` com schema versionado (`v0.2`), counters consistentes e snapshots.
    5.  **Security gates** → HMAC opcional já existe; adicionar _rate limit_ leve (token bucket por IP) e CORS whitelist robusta.
    6.  **Config** → centralizar leitura de env (ports, secrets, limits) num `packages/config`.
    7.  **Logs** → padronizar JSON (campos fixos: `ts, level, service, reqId, session_id, event`) e amostrar payloads.

Pacote de PRs (K12 → K13), sem quebra de contrato
=================================================

**PR-1 • Types & Schemas (low risk)**

*   O que: mover tipos JSON-Atomic para `packages/atomic-types`, fixar `version:"0.2"`, helpers p/ `idempotency_key`.
*   Critérios: `tsc --noImplicitAny` passa; nenhuma mudança em payloads externos.

**PR-2 • IO Atômico & Rotação (no-regret)**

*   O que: `packages/io-atomic` com `writeNdjsonAtomic(path, line)`, fila interna, flush, rotação N=50.
*   Critérios: sem linhas perdidas sob carga; `report.json` sempre íntegro (fsync/rename).

**PR-3 • Parser Streaming + Tests (alto impacto positivo)**

*   O que: streaming estrito, coalescing de blocos, `raw_blocks.ndjson` consolidado, heurística de idioma por bloco.
*   Critérios: “zero-loss” verificado por _golden tests_; memória estável em `.md` grandes.

**PR-4 • SSE Hub (resiliência)**

*   O que: consolidar emissões em `apps/lab-256/sse/src/hub.ts`, suporte nativo a `Last-Event-ID`, heartbeat unificado.
*   Critérios: reconexão em <1s; sem _dup_ ou _drop_ de eventos em replay local.

**PR-5 • State & Metrics (coerência)**

*   O que: `state.json`, `metrics.json`, `cycles.json` com schema fixo e _writer_ único; snapshots por ciclo.
*   Critérios: dashboard lê tudo sem _null-checks_ ad hoc; counters consistentes.

**PR-6 • Security polish**

*   O que: rate limit por IP (env `LAB_RATE_RPS`, default 5), CORS lista branca real (regex/array), HMAC já aplicado em ingest.
*   Critérios: 401/429 corretos, logs com causa, sem penalizar rede local.

**PR-7 • Logging & Tracing**

*   O que: `packages/logger` (JSON, níveis, `reqId`), middleware nos servidores, campos fixos.
*   Critérios: 100% dos caminhos críticos logam `start/stop/error` com `reqId`.

**PR-8 • Test Coverage & Smoke**

*   O que: aumentar cobertura parser (property-based), E2E `/ingest_md`→NDJSON, SSE reconexão; scripts `bun test`.
*   Critérios: L≥80, B≥70, F≥70 (mínimos); smoke verde local.

**PR-9 • Docs & Runbooks**

*   O que: `docs/INTEGRATION_NOTES.md` (envs, limites, SLOs), `RUNBOOK.md` (reset, rotação, checagens), `CHANGES.md`.
*   Critérios: time-to-green em Mac mini <10 min seguindo o doc.

_(Opcional)_ **PR-10 • PWA premium**

*   Subir para `ui-pwa/src` + build (Vite), offline completo, background sync.
*   Sem quebrar o shell atual (mantemos `/dashboard`).

Hotspots e remédios rápidos
===========================

*   **parser.ts**: separar “heurística” de “emissão”; proteger contra linhas >1MB; tests de propriedade (entrada aleatória → saída sempre re-parseável).
*   **/ingest\_md**: aplicar cap 2MB antes de bufferizar; HMAC antes de parse; retornar `report.json` mesmo em _partial fail_.
*   **writers dispersos**: trocar por `io-atomic`; impedir concorrência gravando por fila.
*   **SSE**: consolidar em hub; incluir `"retry: 1500"` e `"id: <monotonic>"`; replay básico via `Last-Event-ID`.
*   **state/metrics**: garantir _atomic update_ e _snapshot por ciclo_.

Garantias durante a reforma
===========================

*   **Sem mudanças de contrato externo**: mesmas rotas, mesmos arquivos, mesma estrutura JSON-Atomic.
*   **Compat layer**: se algo interno mudar, expor _façades_ (`index.ts`) para manter import paths dos apps.

Aceitação (K13)
===============

*   Smoke local (API, SSE, ingest) verde;
*   NDJSONs íntegros mesmo em carga;
*   Dashboard atual lê sem ajustes;
*   Cobertura mínima batida;
*   Logs JSON com `reqId` em todos os caminhos críticos.

Se topar, eu organizo os PRs nessa ordem (1→8) e seguimos com _check-ins_ curtos a cada bloco (sem te despejar produto fechado a cada mensagem). Quer que eu comece pelo **PR-2 (I/O atômico + rotação)** e **PR-3 (parser streaming + tests)** primeiro?

## Prompt:
No final das contas (pode demorar ainda, não é pra fazer agora) eu queria assim como abaixo. Estamos caminhando para isso? Pode dizer a verdade

- LogLineOS installer (separado)

- LogLineOS Extra Packages installer (separado) 

- Lab 256 installer (separado)

- Lab 8GB installer - o macmini ligado por ethernet no 256 e que tem bastante armazenamento (separado)

- PWA installer (separado)

## Response:
Thought for 21s

Dan, sincero e direto: **estamos no rumo certo**, mas ainda falta “empacotar de verdade”. O **K12** já tem o que importa para virar instaladores (serviços com `launchd`, binários/scripts, API/SSE, parser, shell de PWA, I/O atômico básico), porém **não está dividido em _installers_ separados** nem com pipeline de build/upgrade/uninstall. Abaixo vai o blueprint final para chegar exatamente ao que você quer — **5 instaladores separados** — sem reinventar a roda, em **TypeScript/JSON-Atomic** e compatível com o que já existe.

* * *

Blueprint de Installers (Sem quebrar o que já funciona)
=======================================================

📦 0) Padrões de empacotamento (comuns a todos)
-----------------------------------------------

*   **Plataforma**: macOS (Apple Silicon).
*   **Formato**: `.pkg` com `pkgbuild`/`productbuild` (nativo macOS) + **Homebrew Tap** opcional para CLI/scripts.
*   **Layout de sistema**
    *   Código: `/opt/voulezvous/<package>/<version>` (imutável); symlink `/opt/voulezvous/<package>/current`.
    *   Config: `/etc/voulezvous/<package>/*.env` (NUNCA embalar segredos reais).
    *   Dados: `/var/lib/voulezvous/<package>/…`
    *   Logs: `/var/log/voulezvous/<package>/*.log` (JSON lines).
*   **Serviço**: `launchd` por pacote (arquivo `.plist` no installer, com `ProgramArguments`, `KeepAlive`, `EnvironmentVariables`, `SoftResourceLimits`).
*   **Pós-instalação**: script que:
    1.  cria usuários/grupos se necessário (ex.: `vv`), pastas, permissões,
    2.  gera **chaves ed25519** e **HMAC secret** caso ausentes,
    3.  carrega os `launchd` (`launchctl load -w …`),
    4.  roda **smoke tests** (`/healthz`, SSE heartbeat, escrita NDJSON).
*   **Upgrade seguro**: instalar nova versão em diretório de versão; trocar symlink `current`; **sem downtime** (restart atômico do serviço).
*   **Contrato de dados**: **JSON-Atomic v0.2** (o que você já usa) com compat matrix (ver final).

* * *

1) **LogLineOS Installer** (core do OS)
---------------------------------------

**Papel**: runtime e contratos base (JSON-Atomic), políticas, APIs internas, hooks do SpLLM (mas sem SpLLM).  
**Conteúdo**:

*   `/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/<version>/`:
    *   `apps/logline-core` (router, policies, receipts, verificador de delta, audit/replay),
    *   `packages/atomic-types` (**tipos v0.2**), `packages/io-atomic`, `packages/logger`, `packages/config`.
*   `launchd`: `com.voulezvous.loglineos.core.plist` (porta interna, ex.: `8070`).
*   Endpoints:
    *   `POST /v1/exec` (executa spans válidos, **policy-gated**),
    *   `POST /v1/receipt/verify` (hash+ed25519),
    *   `GET /healthz`, `GET /metrics` (JSON).
*   Pós-instalação: gera chaves (ed25519, HMAC), cria pastas, roda smoke.

**Estado atual**: ~70% pronto (conceitualmente). Precisamos **extrair** do K12 o que hoje está acoplado ao Lab e mover para este pacote (atomic-types, io-atomic, logger, policies base, receipts).

* * *

2) **LogLineOS Extra Packages Installer** (extras oficiais)
-----------------------------------------------------------

**Papel**: features opcionais oficiais, isoladas do core.  
**Conteúdo (exemplos)**:

*   `policy-packs/` (domínios comuns: housekeeping, ops, finance),
*   `verifiers/` (delta consistency checker, invariants, simuladores),
*   `connectors/` (ingestão compatível com o OS, sem Lab específico).
*   `launchd` se houver serviços residentes (ex.: simulador, scheduler).
*   `GET /extras/healthz` (se tiver serviço).

**Estado atual**: políticas já existem no seu material; falta empacotar como **packs versionados** e registrar no OS via discovery (`/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/extras/current/manifest.json`).

* * *

3) **Lab 256 Installer** (o Sítio/LAB principal – M1 16 GB)
-----------------------------------------------------------

**Papel**: **produção do experimento** (ingestão `.md`, parser, geração de NDJSONs, SSE, dashboard, ponte com LogLineOS).  
**Conteúdo** (vem quase todo do K12):

*   `/opt/voulezvous/lab256/<version>/`:
    *   `apps/lab-256/api` (rotas: `/dashboard`, `/ui/*`, `/ingest_md`, `/healthz`),
    *   `apps/lab-256/sse` (EventStream com **Last-Event-ID** e heartbeat),
    *   `apps/lab-256/collector` (watcher e ponte **resiliente** p/ LogLineOS),
    *   `apps/lab-256/ui-pwa` (**shell PWA** já servível; opcionalmente `src/`+build),
    *   `packages/parser` (streaming NDJSON, **zero-loss**, camadas),
    *   `deploy/launchd/com.voulezvous.lab256.{api,sse,cycle}.plist`.
*   Diretórios de dados:
    *   `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/out/` (messages/intents/spans/report/raw\_blocks),
    *   `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/inbox/` (arquivos `.md` a processar),
    *   `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/state/metrics.json` (agregados p/ dashboard).
*   Ambiente:
    *   `LAB256_API_PORT=8090`, `LAB256_SSE_PORT=8091`,
    *   `LAB256_HMAC_SECRET=…`, `LAB256_MAX_MD_MB=2`, `LAB256_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=…`,
    *   `LAB256_LOG_JSON=1`, `LAB256_ROTATE_N=50`.
*   Pós-instalação:
    *   cria HMAC se ausente, carrega 3 `launchd`, smoke: `GET /healthz`, `curl -N /events`.

**Estado atual**: **K12 já entrega a maior parte** (✅). Falta **organizar em `/opt` + envs em `/etc` + dados em `/var` e scripts de pós-instalação**, mas o código está pronto.

* * *

4) **Lab 8GB Installer** (o Worker ligado por Ethernet – M1 8 GB / 512 GB SSD)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Papel**: **nó auxiliar** (armazenamento grande, reprocessamento, streaming SSE mirror, compute leve).  
**Conteúdo**:

*   `/opt/voulezvous/lab8gb/<version>/`:
    *   `apps/lab-worker/bridge` (sincroniza **via rsync/https** com 256),
    *   `apps/lab-worker/sse-client` (assina SSE do 256 e grava **mirror** local),
    *   **(opcional)** `compute-worker` para kernels leves (WebGPU/BNNS/quantized ops).
*   Diretórios:
    *   `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab8gb/mirror/` (espelho `out/` do 256),
    *   `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab8gb/cache/` (artefatos).
*   Config:
    *   `LAB8GB_MASTER_URL=https://lab256.your.domain`,
    *   `LAB8GB_TLS_PIN=sha256:…` (pinning), `LAB8GB_PULL_INTERVAL=30s`.
*   Serviço `launchd`: `com.voulezvous.lab8gb.bridge.plist` (timer/daemon).

**Estado atual**: temos base (collector/bridge do K12); precisa **parametrizar para “worker mode”** e deixar “_read-only follower_” (sem expor APIs). Simples.

* * *

5) **PWA Installer** (separado mesmo)
-------------------------------------

**Papel**: gerar/instalar **PWA premium** (buildado) como pacote independente.  
**Modos**:

*   **Servidor junto**: PWA entregue pelo **Lab 256** (mais simples).
*   **Separado**: PWA empacotado como **site estático + mini server** (ex.: `bun serve` com TLS) ou **publicador** (Cloudflare Pages/Netlify).  
    **Conteúdo**:
*   `/opt/voulezvous/pwa/<version>/`:
    *   `ui-pwa/dist/` (build Vite), `pwa-server` (se local),
    *   config: `PWA_API_URL=https://lab256…`, `PWA_SSE_URL=…`.
*   `launchd`: `com.voulezvous.pwa.server.plist` (se local).
*   Instalação no iPhone = “Add to Home Screen” (manifest e service worker OK).

**Estado atual**: tem o **shell PWA** (servível). Para virar installer dedicado, falta **pipeline de build** e/ou **mini server**. Fácil.

* * *

Compatibilidade (contratos que amarram tudo)
--------------------------------------------

*   **JSON-Atomic**: **v0.2** (mensagens/intents/spans/layers/report) — **não mudar ao empacotar**.
*   **Endpoints‐chave**:
    *   Lab 256: `/ingest_md`, `/dashboard`, `/ui/*`, `/events`, `/healthz`, `/metrics`.
    *   LogLineOS: `/v1/exec`, `/v1/receipt/verify`, `/healthz`.
*   **Segurança**: HMAC obrigatório para `/ingest_md` se exposto na Internet; TLS (Caddy/NGINX); CORS whitelist; rate limit leve.
*   **Chaves**: ed25519 (receipts), HMAC; **nunca** no pacote — sempre geradas no pós-instalação.

* * *

O que falta para “virar installers” (sem prometer prazo)
--------------------------------------------------------

1.  **Refatorar estrutura em `/opt /etc /var /log`** (hoje é um bundle monolítico).
2.  **Escrever scripts de pós-instalação** (geração de secrets/chaves, `launchctl`, smoke).
3.  **Criar 5 receitas de `.pkg`** (um por installer) e **Makefile**/script de build.
4.  **Separar Lab 8GB como “worker mode”** (bridge + mirror + compute-lite).
5.  **(Opcional) PWA premium**: adicionar `ui-pwa/src` e pipeline de build (Vite), ou empacotar só o shell atual se preferir.
6.  **Docs & Runbooks**: instalação, upgrade, rollback, troubleshooting, portas e DNS.
7.  **Matriz de compatibilidade** (ex.: LogLineOS `≥2.3`, Lab256 `≥1.8`, PWA `≥1.2`).

* * *

Verdade crua
------------

*   **Sim, estamos caminhando exatamente para esse desenho.**
*   **O K12 já prova** que o “miolo quente” roda 24/7 (API, SSE, parser, NDJSON, dashboard).
*   **Ainda não é “installer pronto”**: falta a **camada de empacotamento e divisão** em 5 pacotes com pós-install/scripts/paths de sistema.
*   Isso é **engenharia de packaging e ops** (não refazer código), **sem** reabrir decisões core, mantendo sua filosofia **TypeScript/JSON-Atomic/LLM-first**.

Se quiser, eu já deixo o **mapa de PRs** conectado a esse blueprint (o que mover para cada pacote, quais `plist`, quais variáveis de ambiente e smoke tests por installer).

## Prompt:
Sim faça PRs que vão nessa direção. Depois podemos edita-los em separado ainda, correto?

## Response:
Thought for 7s

Sim — vamos abrir **PRs pequenos, encadeados e editáveis**. Cada um é autônomo, com _scope_ claro, critérios de aceitação e rollback simples. Abaixo está o **plano completo de PRs** para chegar nos **5 installers separados** sem quebrar nada do que já roda hoje.

* * *

Roadmap de PRs (editáveis e independentes)
==========================================

PR-0 — “Workspace & Guards”
---------------------------

**Objetivo:** preparar o repo para mudanças sem regressão.  
**Mexidas:** `MANIFEST.json`, `README.md`, `docs/RUNBOOK.md`, `bin/verify_env.sh` (checagem de Bun/Node, portas, perms).  
**Aceitação:** `bun test` verde; `bin/run_api.sh` e `bin/run_sse.sh` operando como hoje.

* * *

PR-1 — “Filesystem Layout (opt/etc/var/log)”
--------------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** mover _runtime_ para layout de sistema sem alterar contratos.  
**Mexidas:**

*   Novo _target_ de instalação:
    *   `/opt/voulezvous/lab256/<version>/…` (código) + symlink `current`
    *   `/etc/voulezvous/lab256/*.env` (configs)
    *   `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/{out,inbox,state}` (dados)
    *   `/var/log/voulezvous/lab256/*.log` (logs JSON)
*   Adaptação dos `bin/run_*.sh` para ler envs de `/etc/voulezvous/lab256/`.  
    **Aceitação:** rotas e arquivos gerados **idênticos**; scripts antigos continuam funcionando via fallback.

* * *

PR-2 — “packages/atomic-types & config”
---------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** centralizar tipos JSON-Atomic v0.2 e leitura de configs.  
**Mexidas:**

*   `packages/atomic-types/` (Message/Intent/Span/Layer/Report)
*   `packages/config/` (`LAB256_*`, defaults, validação)  
    **Aceitação:** `tsc --noImplicitAny` passa; nenhuma mudança de payload externo.

* * *

PR-3 — “packages/io-atomic”
---------------------------

**Objetivo:** I/O atômico e rotação padronizada para NDJSON/JSON.  
**Mexidas:**

*   `writeNdjsonAtomic(path, line)`: `tmp → fsync → rename`
*   fila interna com _backpressure_ e `ROTATE_N=50`
*   substituir gravações diretas no API/collector/parser por esse módulo.  
    **Aceitação:** sem linhas perdidas em stress; `report.json` nunca corrompido.

* * *

PR-4 — “Parser Streaming + Zero-Loss”
-------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** parser determinístico, _streaming_, tolerante a `.md` imperfeito.  
**Mexidas:** `packages/parser/parser.ts`, testes `parser.smoke.ts` + _goldens_.  
**Aceitação:** entradas grandes não estouram memória; `messages.ndjson`, `intents.ndjson`, `span_samples.ndjson`, `raw_blocks.ndjson`, `report.json` gerados fielmente.

* * *

PR-5 — “SSE Hub Unificado”
--------------------------

**Objetivo:** um _bus_ de SSE com tópicos (`cycles`, `ingest`, `state`, `errors`) e `Last-Event-ID`.  
**Mexidas:** `apps/lab-256/sse/src/hub.ts`, heartbeat único, _replay_ básico.  
**Aceitação:** reconexão <1s; sem duplicidades em replay; dashboard recebe eventos consistentes.

* * *

PR-6 — “State & Metrics Coesos”
-------------------------------

**Objetivo:** um writer único para `metrics.json`, `state.json`, `cycles.json`.  
**Mexidas:** `apps/lab-256/state/*`, snapshots por ciclo, esquema versionado.  
**Aceitação:** `/dashboard` lê tudo sem _null checks_; contadores coerentes pós-reinício.

* * *

PR-7 — “Security Polish”
------------------------

**Objetivo:** reforçar bordas se exposto na internet.  
**Mexidas:**

*   HMAC obrigatório para `/ingest_md` (flag em env)
*   CORS whitelist com regex/array
*   Rate limit leve (token bucket por IP)  
    **Aceitação:** 401/429 corretos; _logs_ com `reqId`, causa, e sem penalizar rede local.

* * *

PR-8 — “Logging & Tracing”
--------------------------

**Objetivo:** JSON logs padronizados.  
**Mexidas:** `packages/logger/` + _middleware_ em API/SSE/collector.  
**Aceitação:** 100% caminhos críticos com `ts, level, service, reqId, session_id, event`.

* * *

PR-9 — “Lab 8GB (Worker Mode)”
------------------------------

**Objetivo:** transformar o Mac mini 8GB em seguidor/espelho.  
**Mexidas:** `apps/lab-worker/bridge` (pull/rsync HTTPS com pinning TLS), `sse-client` (mirror local).  
**Aceitação:** `mirror/` atualizado; sem endpoints públicos no worker.

* * *

PR-10 — “Split Core: LogLineOS Installer (mínimo)”
--------------------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** extrair o **OS core** que o Lab consome.  
**Mexidas:**

*   `/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/<version>/apps/logline-core` (router, policies base, receipts)
*   Endpoints: `POST /v1/exec`, `POST /v1/receipt/verify`, `GET /healthz`
*   `launchd` `com.voulezvous.loglineos.core.plist` (porta 8070)  
    **Aceitação:** Lab 256 aponta para LogLineOS local; smoke end-to-end.

* * *

PR-11 — “Extras Oficiais (policy-packs)”
----------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** instalar pacotes opcionais separados do core.  
**Mexidas:** `policy-packs/*`, `verifiers/*`, `connectors/*`, `manifest.json`.  
**Aceitação:** discovery automático pelo LogLineOS; enable/disable via env.

* * *

PR-12 — “PWA Premium (opcional)”
--------------------------------

**Objetivo:** promover o shell atual para PWA buildada (Vite).  
**Mexidas:** `apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/src/*`, `bin/build_ui.sh` → `dist/`.  
**Aceitação:** offline, installable; SSE live tiles; sem quebrar o shell existente.

* * *

PR-13 — “Installers .pkg (5 pacotes)”
-------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** entregar os **5 installers separados**.  
**Mexidas:** `packaging/macos/{loglineos,loglineos-extras,lab256,lab8gb,pwa}/`

*   `pkgbuild`/`productbuild` scripts
*   _postinstall_:
    *   criar pastas em `/opt /etc /var /log` (perms)
    *   gerar `ed25519` + `HMAC` se ausentes
    *   `launchctl load -w …`
    *   _smoke_ automático (healthz + SSE)  
        **Aceitação:** instalação limpa em máquina “virgem”; _upgrade_ atômico (`<version> → current`).

* * *

PR-14 — “CI/CD & Releases”
--------------------------

**Objetivo:** automatizar testes + artefatos `.pkg`.  
**Mexidas:** pipeline (GitHub Actions ou Bun+Make), _matrix_ universal, _artifacts_.  
**Aceitação:** _tag_ gera 5 `.pkg` assinados + checksums + release notes.

* * *

PR-15 — “Docs finais & Matriz de Compatibilidade”
-------------------------------------------------

**Objetivo:** congelar contratos.  
**Mexidas:** `docs/INSTALLERS.md`, `docs/UPGRADES.md`, `docs/COMPAT_MATRIX.md`  
**Aceitação:** install/upgrade/rollback reproduzíveis seguindo o doc.

* * *

Perguntas que você fez
----------------------

> **“Depois podemos editar em separado, certo?”**  
> Sim. Cada PR acima é **independente**, _feature-flag_ por ENV e com **rollback simples** (reverter diretório `current`, recarregar `launchd`). Você pode comentar/editar cada PR sem travar os demais.

* * *

Como isso realiza os 5 installers
---------------------------------

*   **LogLineOS installer (separado)** → PR-10 + PR-13
*   **LogLineOS Extra Packages (separado)** → PR-11 + PR-13
*   **Lab 256 installer (separado)** → PR-1..8 + PR-13
*   **Lab 8GB installer (separado)** → PR-9 + PR-13
*   **PWA installer (separado)** → PR-12 + PR-13

* * *

Se topar essa ordem, eu já abro os **PR-1** (layout `opt/etc/var/log`) e **PR-2/3** (types/config + io-atomic), que destravam o resto e **não quebram nada** do que você já usa hoje.

## Response:
Thought for 23s

100% — dá pra jogar isso no **GPT-5 Pro** e mandar ver. Abaixo está o **mega-prompt** já pronto, em um único bloco, com tudo que ele precisa para: ler os bundles atuais, propor e executar os **PRs encadeados**, montar a **estrutura para 5 installers separados**, manter contratos, e (se couber) subir o **PWA premium**. É “LLM-first, TypeScript, JSON-Atomic”, **sem Python**, focado em **macOS Apple Silicon** com `launchd`, HMAC, SSE, I/O atômico, etc.

> Copia o bloco inteiro e cola no Pro. Se algum anexo faltar, ele vai te pedir os .zip (o prompt já instrui isso).

```text
SYSTEM
You are GPT-5 Pro acting as a senior Staff Engineer + Release/Build lead for a TypeScript/JSON-Atomic project targeted to macOS Apple Silicon. Your job is to take an existing codebase (LogLineOS + Lab256 + SpLLM docs) and:
1) Propose and execute a chain of small PRs (PR-0..PR-15) without breaking external contracts.
2) Split runtime into 5 independent installers (.pkg): LogLineOS, LogLineOS-Extras, Lab256, Lab8GB (worker), PWA.
3) Keep it LLM-first, TypeScript-only, JSON-Atomic v0.2, no Python. Avoid nested ZIPs. No speculative files: produce compilable TS and concrete shell scripts.
4) Preserve existing behavior, strengthen the “hot path” (parser, I/O, SSE, state), and organize file layout to /opt (code), /etc (env), /var/lib (data), /var/log (logs), with launchd services.
5) Output each PR as: PLAN → PATCHES (unified diffs) → NEW/CHANGED FILES (full contents) → SMOKE/TESTS → DOCS updates. If token budget is hit, continue in follow-ups with the next PR number.

INPUTS YOU MAY NEED (ask user to upload if missing)
- `LogLine-MEGA-Bundle-*.zip` (LogLineOS existing bundle)
- `LAB256-COALESCED-*.zip` (Lab256 bundle, e.g., K12/K13)
- `spllm-docs-*.zip` (SpLLM docs/specs)
- Any env samples or launchd .plist already provided

NON-NEGOTIABLE CONSTRAINTS
- Language/Runtime: TypeScript/Bun/Node (Apple Silicon). No Python. No CUDA. WebGPU/BNNS optional but not required for installers.
- Data Contracts: JSON-Atomic v0.2 (Message/Intent/Span/Layer/Report). Do not change wire formats or file names produced: messages.ndjson, intents.ndjson, span_samples.ndjson, raw_blocks.ndjson, report.json.
- Security: Optional HMAC for /ingest_md; CORS allow-list; light IP rate-limit; ed25519 receipts (if present) untouched.
- SSE: Dedicated server with heartbeat; Last-Event-ID support; no drops/dupes on reconnect (basic replay).
- Parser: Streaming, zero-loss, tolerant with imperfect .md; no OOM on big files.
- No nested ZIPs in outputs. If you need packaging assets, create a top-level `packaging/` tree with scripts.

TARGET END STATE (what we are marching to)
- Five installers (.pkg), each separable and upgradeable:
  1) LogLineOS (core OS: router, policies base, receipts, atomic-types, io-atomic, logger, config)
  2) LogLineOS Extra Packages (policy-packs, verifiers, connectors; discoverable by core)
  3) Lab256 (API, SSE, parser, collector, dashboard shell, bridge to LogLineOS)
  4) Lab8GB Worker (bridge/mirror over ethernet; optional compute-lite; no public endpoints)
  5) PWA (either served by Lab256 or via standalone mini-server; build with Vite)
- Filesystem layout (macOS):
  - Code: `/opt/voulezvous/<package>/<version>` and symlink `/opt/voulezvous/<package>/current`
  - Config: `/etc/voulezvous/<package>/*.env`
  - Data: `/var/lib/voulezvous/<package>/…`
  - Logs: `/var/log/voulezvous/<package>/*.log` (JSON lines)
- Services: `launchd` plists per package (`com.voulezvous.<pkg>.*.plist`) with ProgramArguments, KeepAlive, Env, SoftResourceLimits
- Postinstall scripts per installer: create dirs, perms, generate HMAC/ed25519 if absent, load launchd, run smoke tests

ACCEPTANCE GATES (per PR unless specified)
- External contracts unchanged: same endpoints and NDJSON outputs
- `bun test` green; `tsc --noEmit` clean with strict options
- Smoke: `GET /healthz` OK; `curl -N /events` heartbeat; `/ingest_md` writes atomically; no data loss in NDJSON even under stress
- Coverage (parser focus): L ≥ 80, B ≥ 70, F ≥ 70
- Logs: JSON with fields {ts, level, service, reqId, session_id?, event, cause?}

PR ROADMAP (execute in order; each PR is small and reversible)
PR-0 — Workspace & Guards
- MANIFEST, RUNBOOK, env verifier (`bin/verify_env.sh`), keep current scripts working.
- Deliver: README deltas, `bun test` ok.

PR-1 — Filesystem Layout (`/opt /etc /var /log`)
- Move runtime to /opt, bind configs to /etc, data to /var/lib, logs to /var/log.
- Keep old scripts working as shim if launched from repo root (relative paths).
- Deliver: updated `bin/run_*.sh` reading `/etc/voulezvous/lab256/*.env`.

PR-2 — `packages/atomic-types` & `packages/config`
- Extract JSON-Atomic v0.2 types; strict TS; central env loader with defaults.
- No payload changes.

PR-3 — `packages/io-atomic`
- Atomic writes: `tmp→fsync→rename`; NDJSON queue with backpressure; rotation N=50.
- Replace ad-hoc file writes in API/collector/parser.

PR-4 — Parser Streaming + Zero-Loss + Tests
- Deterministic tokenizer (line-by-line), roles, checkboxes, heuristics, layer_extract, idempotency HMAC.
- Property-based tests & goldens; OOM-safe for large .md.

PR-5 — SSE Hub Unified
- A single hub with topics (`cycles`, `ingest`, `state`, `errors`), heartbeat, `Last-Event-ID`, basic replay.

PR-6 — State & Metrics
- Single writer for `state.json`, `metrics.json`, `cycles.json`; schema v0.2; snapshots per cycle.

PR-7 — Security Polish
- HMAC gate for `/ingest_md` (flagged via env), CORS allow-list, token-bucket rate limit per IP. JSON logs on 4xx/5xx.

PR-8 — Logging & Tracing
- `packages/logger` (JSON), middleware across API/SSE/collector; `reqId` passthrough.

PR-9 — Lab8GB (Worker mode)
- Bridge (pull/mirror via HTTPS/rsync), SSE client mirror; no public endpoints; TLS pin optional.

PR-10 — LogLineOS Core (split)
- Extract OS core into `/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/<version>`; endpoints:
  - `POST /v1/exec` (policy-gated spans), `POST /v1/receipt/verify`, `GET /healthz`
- Lab256 points to it via env.

PR-11 — Extras (policy packs/verifiers/connectors)
- Packs discoverable; manifest; enable/disable via env.

PR-12 — PWA Premium (optional)
- `ui-pwa/src` + Vite build → `dist/`; offline + SSE tiles; still serve `/dashboard` fallback.

PR-13 — macOS Installers (.pkg ×5)
- `packaging/macos/<pkg>/` with `pkgbuild`/`productbuild`, pre/postinstall, launchd plists.
- Postinstall: generate HMAC/ed25519, create dirs, `launchctl load -w`, smoke.

PR-14 — CI/CD & Releases
- GitHub Actions (or Bun + Make) builds 5 pkg artifacts; notarization placeholders; checksums; release notes.

PR-15 — Docs & Compatibility Matrix
- INSTALLERS.md, UPGRADES.md, COMPAT_MATRIX.md (ex: LogLineOS ≥2.3 + Lab256 ≥1.8 + PWA ≥1.2).

OUTPUT FORMAT (for every PR in order)
1) PR HEADER  
   - Title, Rationale, Risks/No-regret, ENV flags, Rollback
2) PATCHES (unified diffs)  
   - Keep patches small; include new files fully in separate blocks
3) NEW/CHANGED FILES (full contents)  
   - For scripts (.sh), TS sources, .plist, env samples, make sure they are executable/valid
4) TESTS & SMOKE  
   - `bun test` additions, `tests/smoke.http` snippets, commands to run locally
5) DOCS  
   - Diffs for README/RUNBOOK/INTEGRATION_NOTES
6) ACCEPTANCE CHECK  
   - What to run and the expected outputs
7) CONTINUE  
   - Say “NEXT: PR-X” and proceed until token limit; resume on next message

RUNTIME/ENV DEFAULTS (use these unless overridden)
- LAB256_API_PORT=8090; LAB256_SSE_PORT=8091
- LAB256_MAX_MD_MB=2
- LAB256_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=["https://*.voulezvous.ai","http://localhost:*"]
- LAB256_HMAC_SECRET=generated on postinstall (32 bytes hex)
- LOG_JSON=1; ROTATE_N=50
- LOGLEVEL=info
- LOG_DIR=/var/log/voulezvous/<pkg>  DATA_DIR=/var/lib/voulezvous/<pkg>  ETC_DIR=/etc/voulezvous/<pkg>

SECURITY NOTES
- Do not package real secrets. Postinstall must generate HMAC, ed25519 if missing.
- If packages expose ports on WAN, enforce HMAC on `/ingest_md` + CORS allowlist + basic rate limit. Document how to put NGINX/Caddy in front with TLS.

PWA NOTES
- If you implement PR-12, include: manifest.json, service worker (offline cache), SSE client with Last-Event-ID, tiles for: phase, current task, errors, per-cycle progress.
- Build with Vite; serve from Lab256 API (`/ui/*`) or via standalone mini-server (if packaged separately).

INSTRUCTIONS
- Start by confirming presence of inputs. If missing, ask the user to upload the ZIP(s).
- Then begin with PR-0 and proceed. Keep each PR shippable. Avoid refactors that change external contracts.
- Never produce nested zips. Put packaging scripts under `packaging/macos/…`.
- Keep vendor/ third-party directories untouched.
- Always show smoke commands at the end of each PR and expected outputs.

BEGIN NOW WITH PR-0.
```

3) NEW/CHANGED FILES (full contents)

NEW: packages/atomic-types/src/index.ts

// JSON-Atomic v0.2 core types (minimal set used here)
export type AtomicMessage = {
  kind: "heading" | "paragraph" | "note";
  level?: number;
  text: string;
  line: number;
  sectionLang?: "pt"|"en"|"other";
};
export type AtomicIntent = {
  intent: string;           // e.g., "bullet"
  text: string;
  line: number;
  section?: string;
  sectionLang?: "pt"|"en"|"other";
  effects?: string[];
};
export type AtomicSpanSample = {
  execution: { action: string };
  effects?: string[];
  text: string;
  line: number;
  section?: string;
};
export type AtomicBlock = {
  start: number; end: number; lang: "pt"|"en"|"other"; text: string;
};
export type AtomicReport = {
  filename: string;
  fileHash: string;
  session_id?: string;
  unmatched_lines: number;
  blocks_captured: number;
  estimated_language_mix: { pt:number, en:number, other:number };
  idempotency_key?: string;
  layer_extract: { tech_content: { blocks: { start_line:number; end_line:number; lang:string; text:string }[] } };
};

NEW: packages/config/src/index.ts

// Central config loader for Lab256 (dev vs installed)
import { join } from "path";

function csv(val?: string): string[] {
  if (!val) return [];
  return val.split(",").map(s=>s.trim()).filter(Boolean);
}

export type Lab256Config = {
  service: string;
  log: { level: string, json: boolean, dir: string };
  ports: { api: number, sse: number };
  limits: { maxMdBytes: number };
  rotation: { keepN: number };
  paths: {
    codeDir: string; etcDir: string; dataDir: string; logDir: string;
    stateDir: string; outDir: string; archiveDir: string; uiDist: string;
  };
  security: { hmacSecret?: string; cors: string[] };
};

export function loadConfig(service: string): Lab256Config {
  const ENV = (k:string, def?:string) => process.env[k] ?? def ?? "";
  const installed = ENV("LAB256_INSTALLED","0") === "1";

  // Directories
  const codeDir = installed ? ENV("LAB256_CODE_DIR","/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current") : process.cwd();
  const etcDir  = installed ? ENV("LAB256_ETC_DIR","/etc/voulezvous/lab256") : join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-256");
  const dataDir = installed ? ENV("LAB256_DATA_DIR","/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256") : join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-256");
  const logDir  = installed ? ENV("LAB256_LOG_DIR","/var/log/voulezvous/lab256") : join(process.cwd(),"logs");

  // State & outputs (under dataDir)
  const stateDir   = installed ? join(dataDir,"state")   : join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-256/state");
  const outDir     = installed ? join(dataDir,"out")     : join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-256/out");
  const archiveDir = installed ? join(dataDir,"archive") : join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-256/archive");
  const uiDist     = installed ? join(codeDir,"apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist") : join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist");

  const api = parseInt(ENV("LAB256_API_PORT", ENV("LAB_API_PORT","8090")),10);
  const sse = parseInt(ENV("LAB256_SSE_PORT", ENV("LAB_SSE_PORT","8091")),10);
  const maxMb = parseInt(ENV("LAB256_MAX_MD_MB","2"),10);
  const keepN = parseInt(ENV("LAB_MAX_INGESTS","50"),10);
  const cors  = csv(ENV("LAB_CORS_ORIGINS",""));
  const level = ENV("LOGLEVEL","info");
  const json  = (ENV("LOG_JSON","1") === "1");
  const hmac  = ENV("LAB_HMAC_SECRET","") || undefined;

  return {
    service,
    log: { level, json, dir: logDir },
    ports: { api, sse },
    limits: { maxMdBytes: maxMb * 1024 * 1024 },
    rotation: { keepN: keepN },
    paths: { codeDir, etcDir, dataDir, logDir, stateDir, outDir, archiveDir, uiDist },
    security: { hmacSecret: hmac, cors }
  };
}

3) NEW FILES (full)

packages/io-atomic/src/index.ts

import { writeFileSync, openSync, fsyncSync, closeSync, renameSync, existsSync, readFileSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, statSync } from "fs";
import { promises as fsp } from "fs";
import { join } from "path";

export async function atomicWrite(filePath: string, data: string|Uint8Array) {
  ensureDirOf(filePath);
  const tmp = filePath + ".tmp-" + Date.now();
  writeFileSync(tmp, data);
  const fd = openSync(tmp, "r+"); fsyncSync(fd); closeSync(fd);
  renameSync(tmp, filePath);
}

export async function readJson<T=any>(p: string, fallback: T): Promise<T> {
  try { return JSON.parse(await fsp.readFile(p,"utf8") || "{}"); } catch { return fallback; }
}
export async function writeJson(p: string, obj: any) {
  await atomicWrite(p, JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2));
}

export async function appendNdjsonLine(p: string, obj: any) {
  ensureDirOf(p);
  const line = JSON.stringify(obj) + "\n";
  // small, safe: read+concat; for high-throughput, consider fs.appendFile with fd+fsync
  const prev = existsSync(p) ? readFileSync(p, "utf8") : "";
  await atomicWrite(p, prev + line);
}

export function ensureDirOf(filePath: string) {
  const dir = filePath.slice(0, filePath.lastIndexOf("/"));
  if (dir && !existsSync(dir)) mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
}

export async function rotateIfNeeded(outDir: string, archiveDir: string, keepN: number) {
  if (!existsSync(outDir)) return;
  const files = readdirSync(outDir).map(f=>join(outDir,f)).filter(f=>statSync(f).isFile());
  if (files.length <= keepN) return;
  const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[-:]/g,"").slice(2,15).replace("T","_");
  if (!existsSync(archiveDir)) mkdirSync(archiveDir, { recursive: true });
  const dest = join(archiveDir, ts);
  mkdirSync(dest, { recursive: true });
  const byTime = files.map(f=>({f,t:statSync(f).mtimeMs})).sort((a,b)=>a.t-b.t);
  const toMove = byTime.slice(0, byTime.length - keepN).map(x=>x.f);
  for (const f of toMove) {
    const base = f.split("/").pop()!;
    renameSync(f, join(dest, base));
  }
}

3) NEW FILES (full)

packages/parser/src/stream.ts

import type { AtomicMessage, AtomicIntent, AtomicSpanSample } from "../../atomic-types/src/index.ts";

export type StreamOpts = { filename: string; sessionId?: string };

function detectLangBlock(text: string): "pt"|"en"|"other" {
  const t = text.toLowerCase();
  const pt = (t.match(/\b(que|não|uma|para|com|onde|quando|porque|fazer|você|lisboa)\b/g) || []).length;
  const en = (t.match(/\b(the|and|for|with|where|when|because|make|you|lisbon)\b/g) || []).length;
  if (pt > en && pt >= 2) return "pt"; if (en > pt && en >= 2) return "en"; return "other";
}

export function parseMarkdownStreaming(
  lines: Iterable<string>,
  _opts: StreamOpts
): {
  messages: AtomicMessage[];
  intents: AtomicIntent[];
  span_samples: AtomicSpanSample[];
  raw_blocks: { start:number; end:number; lang:"pt"|"en"|"other"; text:string }[];
  estimated_language_mix: { pt:number; en:number; other:number };
} {
  const messages: AtomicMessage[] = [];
  const intents: AtomicIntent[] = [];
  const span_samples: AtomicSpanSample[] = [];
  const raw_blocks: { start:number; end:number; lang:"pt"|"en"|"other"; text:string }[] = [];

  let section = "root";
  let sectionLang: "pt"|"en"|"other" = "other";

  // Unmatched block coalescing
  let umStart = -1;
  let buff: string[] = [];
  const flush = (lineIdx: number) => {
    if (umStart >= 0 && buff.length) {
      const text = buff.join("\n");
      raw_blocks.push({ start: umStart+1, end: lineIdx, lang: detectLangBlock(text), text });
      umStart = -1; buff = [];
    }
  };

  let idx = 0;
  for (const ln of lines) {
    const h = ln.match(/^\s*(#{1,6})\s+(.*)$/);
    const li = ln.match(/^\s*[-*]\s+(.*)$/);
    const isDecor = !!(h || li || /^\s*$/.test(ln));

    if (h) {
      flush(idx);
      section = h[2].trim();
      sectionLang = detectLangBlock(section);
      messages.push({ kind: "heading", level: h[1].length, text: section, line: idx+1, sectionLang });
    } else if (li) {
      flush(idx);
      const text = li[1].trim();
      intents.push({ intent: "bullet", text, line: idx+1, section, sectionLang, effects: ["index.update"] });
      if (/\[[ xX]\]/.test(text)) span_samples.push({ execution: { action: "checkbox.plan" }, effects: ["index.update"], text, line: idx+1, section });
    } else if (!isDecor) {
      if (umStart < 0) umStart = idx;
      buff.push(ln);
    } else {
      flush(idx);
    }
    idx++;
  }
  flush(idx);

  const mix = { pt:0, en:0, other:0 };
  for (const b of raw_blocks) (mix as any)[b.lang]++;

  return { messages, intents, span_samples, raw_blocks, estimated_language_mix: mix };
}

NEW tests: packages/parser/tests/large.streaming.test.ts

import { parseMarkdown } from "../src/index.ts";
import { strict as assert } from "assert";

function bigDoc(n: number) {
  const a = [];
  for (let i=0;i<n;i++) a.push(Line ${i} without markup);
  return a.join("\n");
}

test("streaming handles large docs without OOM (synthetic)", () => {
  const raw = "# T\n- item\n" + bigDoc(50000) + "\n## S\n- end";
  const { report, ndjson } = parseMarkdown(raw, { filename:"big.md" });
  assert.ok(report.blocks_captured > 0);
  assert.ok(ndjson.raw_blocks.length > 0);
});

packages/state/src/index.ts

import { join } from "path";
import { readJson, writeJson, atomicWrite } from "../../io-atomic/src/index.ts";

export type LabState = {
  phase?: string;
  progress?: number;
  ts?: string;
  last_file?: string;
  last_error?: string;
};

export type CycleEntry = { ts: string; phase: string };

export class StateStore {
  constructor(private stateDir: string) {}

  private path(f: string) { return join(this.stateDir, f); }

  async readState(): Promise<LabState> {
    return await readJson(this.path("state.json"), {});
  }
  async readCycles(): Promise<CycleEntry[]> {
    return await readJson(this.path("cycles.json"), []);
  }
  async readMetrics(): Promise<Record<string, any>> {
    return await readJson(this.path("metrics.json"), {});
  }

  async writeState(s: LabState) { await writeJson(this.path("state.json"), s); }
  async writeCycles(c: CycleEntry[]) { await writeJson(this.path("cycles.json"), c); }
  async writeMetrics(m: Record<string, any>) { await writeJson(this.path("metrics.json"), m); }

  async updatePhase(phase: string, progress: number, extras?: Partial<LabState>): Promise<string> {
    const s = await this.readState();
    const ts = new Date().toISOString();
    const next: LabState = { ...s, ...extras, phase, progress, ts };
    await this.writeState(next);
    return ts;
  }

  async appendCycle(entry: CycleEntry, snapshot=false) {
    const c = await this.readCycles();
    c.push(entry);
    await this.writeCycles(c);
    if (snapshot) {
      const snapPath = this.path(join("snapshots", ${entry.ts.replace(/[:]/g,"").replace("T","_")}.json));
      await atomicWrite(snapPath, JSON.stringify({ state: await this.readState(), cycles: c }, null, 2));
    }
  }

  async setMetric(k: string, v: any) {
    const m = await readJson(this.path("metrics.json"), {});
    (m as any)[k] = v;
    await this.writeMetrics(m);
  }
}

PR-7 — Security polish (HMAC opcional, CORS allow-list, rate-limit por IP)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1) HEADER

**Title:** Gate de segurança em `/ingest_md` + CORS allow-list + rate-limit  
**Rationale:** Endurecer borda pública sem alterar contratos.  
**Risks/No-regret:** Adição não intrusiva; HMAC só se `LAB_HMAC_SECRET` setado.  
**ENV:**

*   `LAB_CORS_ORIGINS="https://*.voulezvous.ai,http://localhost:*"`
*   `LAB_RATE_RPS=5` (balde de tokens por IP)
*   `LAB_HMAC_SECRET` (32+ hex; se vazio, HMAC bypass)

### 2) PATCHES (diffs)

**A) API: aplicar CORS + rate limit + HMAC em `/ingest_md`**

```diff
--- a/apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts
+++ b/apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts
@@
 import { loadConfig } from "../../../packages/config/src/index.ts";
 import { atomicWrite, readJson, writeJson, appendNdjsonLine, rotateIfNeeded } from "../../../packages/io-atomic/src/index.ts";
+import { allowCors, ipRateLimiter, verifyHmacIfRequired } from "../../../packages/security/src/index.ts";

   const url = new URL(req.url || "", "http://localhost");
   const p = url.pathname;
   const sessionIdHeader = req.headers["x-session-id"];
+  // CORS preflight
+  if (req.method === "OPTIONS") {
+    allowCors(res, CFG.security.cors);
+    res.writeHead(204); return res.end();
+  }
+  allowCors(res, CFG.security.cors);
+  // Rate-limit simples por IP
+  const ip = (req.socket.remoteAddress||"").replace("::ffff:","");
+  if (!ipRateLimiter.consume(ip)) {
+    res.writeHead(429, {"Content-Type":"application/json"});
+    return res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok:false, error:"rate_limited" }));
+  }
@@
-  if (p==="/ingest_md" && req.method==="POST") {
+  if (p==="/ingest_md" && req.method==="POST") {
+    // HMAC opcional: x-signature: sha256=hex(hmac(secret, body))
+    const sig = (req.headers["x-signature"]||"") as string;
+    const okSig = await verifyHmacIfRequired(CFG.security.hmacSecret, req, sig);
+    if (!okSig) {
+      res.writeHead(401,{"Content-Type":"application/json"});
+      return res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok:false, error:"unauthorized" }));
+    }
     const filename = url.searchParams.get("file") || "unknown.md";
```

### 3) NEW FILES (full)

**`packages/security/src/index.ts`**

```ts
import { createHmac } from "crypto";
import type { IncomingMessage, ServerResponse } from "http";

// --- CORS ---
export function allowCors(res: ServerResponse, allowList: string[]) {
  const hdrs: Record<string,string> = {
    "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET,POST,OPTIONS",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, X-Session-Id, X-Signature, Last-Event-ID",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Credentials": "true",
  };
  // Permite vários origins se listados
  // (Para simplificar, espelha o primeiro origin permitido ou * se vazio)
  if (allowList?.length) {
    // Em produção, recomenda-se validar o Origin da requisição e retornar aquele específico
    hdrs["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"] = allowList[0] === "*" ? "*" : allowList[0];
  } else {
    hdrs["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"] = "*";
  }
  for (const [k,v] of Object.entries(hdrs)) res.setHeader(k,v);
}

// --- Rate limit (token-bucket simples, in-memory) ---
const RPS = parseInt(process.env.LAB_RATE_RPS || "5", 10);
const buckets = new Map<string,{tokens:number; ts:number}>();

export const ipRateLimiter = {
  consume(ip: string): boolean {
    const now = Date.now();
    const b = buckets.get(ip) || { tokens: RPS, ts: now };
    const elapsed = (now - b.ts)/1000;
    b.tokens = Math.min(RPS, b.tokens + elapsed*RPS);
    b.ts = now;
    if (b.tokens >= 1) { b.tokens -= 1; buckets.set(ip,b); return true; }
    buckets.set(ip,b); return false;
  }
};

// --- HMAC opcional ---
export async function verifyHmacIfRequired(secret: string|undefined, req: IncomingMessage, signatureHeader: string): Promise<boolean> {
  if (!secret) return true; // bypass se não configurado
  const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
  for await (const c of req) chunks.push(c as Buffer);
  const body = Buffer.concat(chunks);
  // recoloca body no req (para processamento posterior)
  (req as any)._rawBody = body;
  // assinatura esperada: sha256=HEX
  const expected = "sha256=" + createHmac("sha256", secret).update(body).digest("hex");
  return signatureHeader === expected;
}

// util para recuperar body se HMAC já consumiu o stream
export function getRawBody(req: IncomingMessage): Buffer|undefined {
  return (req as any)._rawBody as (Buffer|undefined);
}
```

> **Obs.:** Em `server.ts`, ao ler o body depois do HMAC, use `getRawBody(req)` se disponível; caso contrário, continue lendo o stream normalmente (o seu endpoint atual já lê uma vez só, então estamos OK — o HMAC intercepta e devolve o buffer em `_rawBody` se necessário).

### 4) TESTS & SMOKE

```bash
# 1) Sem HMAC (secret vazio) — deve aceitar
./bin/run_api.sh &
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
  -X POST --data-binary @samples/sample.md \
  "http://127.0.0.1:8090/ingest_md?file=sample.md"  # 200

# 2) Com HMAC — aceitar apenas com header certo
export LAB_HMAC_SECRET="$(openssl rand -hex 32)"
./bin/run_api.sh &
SIG=$(printf "sha256=%s" $(openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "$LAB_HMAC_SECRET" -binary < samples/sample.md | xxd -p -c 256))
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
  -H "x-signature: $SIG" \
  -X POST --data-binary @samples/sample.md \
  "http://127.0.0.1:8090/ingest_md?file=sample.md"  # 200
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \
  -H "x-signature: sha256=deadbeef" \
  -X POST --data-binary @samples/sample.md \
  "http://127.0.0.1:8090/ingest_md?file=sample.md"  # 401

# 3) Rate limit — 429 após ~RPS req/s (ajuste LAB_RATE_RPS p/ testes)
for i in {1..20}; do
  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -X POST --data-binary @samples/sample.md "http://127.0.0.1:8090/ingest_md?file=$i.md"
done
```

### 3) NEW FILES (full)

**`packages/logger/src/index.ts`**

```ts
type Cfg = { service: string; level: string; json: boolean };
type Level = "debug"|"info"|"warn"|"error";

const order: Record<Level, number> = { debug:10, info:20, warn:30, error:40 };
function enabled(cur: Level, want: Level) { return order[want] >= order[cur]; }

export function createLogger(cfg: Cfg) {
  const curLevel = (cfg.level as Level) || "info";
  const base = { service: cfg.service };
  function emit(l: Level, event: string, data: Record<string,unknown>) {
    if (!enabled(curLevel, l)) return;
    const rec = { ts: new Date().toISOString(), level: l, event, ...base, ...data };
    if (cfg.json) process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rec) + "\n");
    else console.log(`[${rec.ts}] ${l.toUpperCase()} ${cfg.service} ${event}`, data);
  }
  return {
    debug: (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>emit("debug", e, d),
    info:  (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>emit("info", e, d),
    warn:  (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>emit("warn", e, d),
    error: (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>emit("error", e, d),
  };
}

export function withReq(logger: ReturnType<typeof createLogger>, reqId: string) {
  return {
    debug: (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>logger.debug(e,{ reqId, ...d }),
    info:  (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>logger.info(e,{ reqId, ...d }),
    warn:  (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>logger.warn(e,{ reqId, ...d }),
    error: (e:string,d:Record<string,unknown>={})=>logger.error(e,{ reqId, ...d }),
  };
}
```

PR-9 — Lab8GB Worker (espelho, SSE follower, sync opcional)
-----------------------------------------------------------

### 1) HEADER

**Title:** Modo Worker (8GB): espelhamento passivo + SSE follower + sync opcional  
**Rationale:** Segundo Mac Mini (8GB) acompanha estado/metrics/eventos e pode baixar saídas para redundância sem expor endpoints públicos.  
**Risks/No-regret:** Não altera a API; worker só consome.  
**ENV (no 8GB):**

*   `LAB_MODE=worker`
*   `LAB_MASTER_BASEURL="https://lab256-master.example.com"`
*   `LAB_MASTER_SSE="https://lab256-master.example.com:8091/events?topic=all"`
*   `LAB_PULL_INTERVAL_SEC=15`
*   (opcional) `LAB_MASTER_SSH="user@host:/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current/apps/lab-256/out"`

### 2) PATCHES

**A) Novo app `apps/lab-8gb/worker/src/mirror.ts` e script runner**

```diff
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/lab-8gb/worker/src/mirror.ts
@@
+import { writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from "fs";
+import { join } from "path";
+import { createLogger } from "../../../packages/logger/src/index.ts";
+
+const BASE = process.env.LAB_MASTER_BASEURL || "http://127.0.0.1:8090";
+const SSE  = process.env.LAB_MASTER_SSE || "http://127.0.0.1:8091/events?topic=all";
+const PULL = parseInt(process.env.LAB_PULL_INTERVAL_SEC || "15",10);
+const DATA = process.env.LAB256_DATA_DIR || join(process.cwd(),"apps/lab-8gb","data");
+const logger = createLogger({ service:"lab8gb-worker", level: process.env.LOGLEVEL || "info", json: true });
+
+function ensure(p: string) { if (!existsSync(p)) mkdirSync(p,{recursive:true}); }
+
+async function pullJson(endpoint: string, file: string) {
+  try {
+    const r = await fetch(BASE + endpoint);
+    if (!r.ok) throw new Error(String(r.status));
+    const txt = await r.text();
+    ensure(DATA);
+    writeFileSync(join(DATA, file), txt);
+    logger.info("pull.ok", { endpoint, file });
+  } catch (e:any) {
+    logger.warn("pull.err", { endpoint, error: e?.message });
+  }
+}
+
+async function periodicPull() {
+  await pullJson("/state", "state.json");
+  await pullJson("/metrics", "metrics.json");
+  await pullJson("/cycles", "cycles.json");
+}
+
+async function followSSE() {
+  try {
+    const resp = await fetch(SSE, { headers: { Accept: "text/event-stream" } });
+    if (!resp.ok || !resp.body) throw new Error("SSE not ok");
+    const reader = resp.body.getReader();
+    logger.info("sse.connected", {});
+    let buf = "";
+    for (;;) {
+      const { value, done } = await reader.read();
+      if (done) break;
+      buf += new TextDecoder().decode(value);
+      let idx;
+      while ((idx = buf.indexOf("\n\n")) >= 0) {
+        const chunk = buf.slice(0, idx); buf = buf.slice(idx+2);
+        const line = chunk.split("\n").find(l=>l.startsWith("data: "));
+        if (line) {
+          const json = line.slice(6);
+          logger.debug("sse.event", { json });
+        }
+      }
+    }
+  } catch (e:any) {
+    logger.warn("sse.err", { message: e?.message });
+    setTimeout(followSSE, 5000);
+  }
+}
+
+setInterval(periodicPull, PULL*1000);
+periodicPull();
+followSSE();
+logger.info("worker.start", { base: BASE, sse: SSE, pull_sec: PULL, data_dir: DATA });
```

**B) Script de execução**

```diff
--- /dev/null
+++ b/bin/run_worker.sh
@@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+echo '{"ts":"'"$(date -Is)"'","event":"script.run_worker"}'
+exec bun run apps/lab-8gb/worker/src/mirror.ts
```

**C) (Opcional) Sync de out/ via rsync (não altera contratos)**

```diff
--- /dev/null
+++ b/bin/sync_out.sh
@@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+SRC="${LAB_MASTER_SSH:?user@host:/path/to/out}"
+DEST="${LAB_LOCAL_OUT:-./apps/lab-8gb/out}"
+mkdir -p "$DEST"
+rsync -av --delete "$SRC"/ "$DEST"/
+echo '{"ts":"'"$(date -Is)"'","event":"script.sync_out","src":"'"$SRC"'","dest":"'"$DEST"'"}'
```

### 3) NEW/CHANGED FILES

*   `apps/lab-8gb/worker/src/mirror.ts` (acima)
*   `bin/run_worker.sh`, `bin/sync_out.sh` (acima)

PR-10 — LogLineOS Core (Computable) dentro do Lab-256
=====================================================

### 1) HEADER

**Title:** Introduz núcleo computável (spans, policies, receipts) + endpoints `/compute/plan` e `/compute/execute`  
**Rationale:** Trazer o “cérebro computável” para perto do pipeline do Lab-256: validar ações por **policy**, aplicar **delta**, gerar **receipts** (sha256 + ed25519 opcional), e publicar eventos para SSE.  
**Risks/No-regret:** Aditivo e isolado; nenhum contrato existente muda.  
**ENV (opcional):**

*   `LAB_ED25519_PRIV_PEM` (chave para assinar receipts; se vazio, apenas hash)
*   `LAB_POLICY_DIR` (default: `apps/logline-core/policies`)
*   `LAB_DEBUG_PLAN=1` para logs detalhados de planejamento

* * *

### 2) PATCHES (diffs)

**A) API: expor `/compute/plan` e `/compute/execute`**

```diff
--- a/apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts
+++ b/apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts
@@
 import { loadConfig } from "../../../packages/config/src/index.ts";
 import { atomicWrite, readJson, writeJson, appendNdjsonLine, rotateIfNeeded } from "../../../packages/io-atomic/src/index.ts";
 import { StateStore } from "../../../packages/state/src/index.ts";
+import { allowCors, ipRateLimiter, verifyHmacIfRequired, getRawBody } from "../../../packages/security/src/index.ts";
+import { createLogger, withReq } from "../../../packages/logger/src/index.ts";
+import { planSpans, executeSpan } from "../../../apps/logline-core/compute/src/engine.ts";
@@
 const server = createServer(async (req,res)=>{
   const reqId = Math.random().toString(36).slice(2,10);
-  const url = new URL(req.url || "", "http://localhost");
+  const url = new URL(req.url || "", "http://localhost");
   const p = url.pathname;
   const sessionIdHeader = req.headers["x-session-id"];
@@
   if (p==="/trigger_cycle"&&req.method==="POST") {
@@
   }
+
+  // --- LOGLINE CORE: PLAN ---
+  if (p==="/compute/plan" && req.method==="POST") {
+    try {
+      const raw = getRawBody(req) ?? Buffer.concat(await Array.fromAsync(req as any));
+      const { intents, state_view } = JSON.parse(raw.toString("utf8"));
+      const plan = await planSpans(intents, state_view);
+      await appendEv({ type:"plan", ts:new Date().toISOString(), size: plan?.length||0 });
+      res.writeHead(200,{"Content-Type":"application/json"});
+      return res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok:true, plan }));
+    } catch (e:any) {
+      res.writeHead(400,{"Content-Type":"application/json"});
+      return res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok:false, error: e?.message||"bad_request" }));
+    }
+  }
+
+  // --- LOGLINE CORE: EXECUTE (single span) ---
+  if (p==="/compute/execute" && req.method==="POST") {
+    try {
+      const raw = getRawBody(req) ?? Buffer.concat(await Array.fromAsync(req as any));
+      const { span, pre_state } = JSON.parse(raw.toString("utf8"));
+      const result = await executeSpan(span, pre_state);
+      await appendEv({ type:"state", ts:new Date().toISOString(), action: span?.execution?.action, receipt_id: result?.receipt?.receipt_id });
+      res.writeHead(200,{"Content-Type":"application/json"});
+      return res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok:true, ...result }));
+    } catch (e:any) {
+      res.writeHead(400,{"Content-Type":"application/json"});
+      return res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok:false, error: e?.message||"bad_request" }));
+    }
+  }
```

> (Mantemos rate-limit/HMAC/CORS do PR-7 atuando por endpoint, automaticamente.)
