gerar

## Response:
Thought for 32s

perfeito. segue o “kit mínimo” **manifest-driven CI/CD** em TypeScript + Bun, pronto pra colar no teu monorepo. inclui tipos do manifesto, gerador de release, assinatura ed25519, CAS local simples e o **Apply-Agent** (dry-run → apply → health → receipts). tudo em **um único snippet** com árvore + arquivos.

> Como usar (resumo):
> 
> 1.  gere tarballs `.tgz` dos 5 instaláveis → 2) rode `build-and-manifest.ts` (assina e publica no CAS local) → 3) rode `apply-agent` no mini com `--mode dry-run` ou `--mode enforce`.  
>     Receipts e eventos vão para `apps/lab-256/state/*.ndjson` (ou `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/*` se instalado).
>     

```ts
// ============================================================================
// FILE TREE (colocar estes arquivos no monorepo; caminhos relativos sugeridos)
// ============================================================================
//
// packages/
//   atomic-release/
//     src/types.ts
//   signing/
//     src/ed25519.ts
//   cas/
//     src/local.ts
//   io-atomic/                    (já existe no teu repo; se não, use o deste snippet)
//     src/index.ts                (opcional: se já tiver, ignore este arquivo)
// apps/
//   apply-agent/
//     src/agent.ts
// scripts/
//   ci/
//     build-and-manifest.ts
// bin/
//   apply_manifest.sh
// etc/
//   voulezvous/
//     trust.json                  (trust root com chave pública)
//     lab256.env.sample           (se já tiver, ignore)
//
// ============================================================================
// FILE: packages/atomic-release/src/types.ts
// ============================================================================
export type ReleaseUnit = {
  name: "loglineos" | "extras" | "lab256" | "lab8gb" | "pwa" | string;
  target_selector: string[]; // ["lab256","lab8gb"]
  artifact: {
    url: string;             // https://... ou file:///...
    sha256: string;          // hex
    size_bytes: number;
  };
  apply: Array<
    | { action: "fs.extract"; to: string }
    | { action: "fs.symlink_flip"; link: string; to: string }
    | { action: "service.restart"; name: string }
  >;
  health?: Array<
    | { check: "http.get"; url: string; expect: any; timeout_ms?: number }
  >;
  policies?: string[];       // ex: ["policy.no_downgrade","policy.disk_free_gte_5gb"]
};

export type ReleaseManifest = {
  type: "release.manifest";
  version: "0.2";
  release_id: string;        // ex: rel_2025-10-17_v19.4
  created_at: string;        // ISO
  units: ReleaseUnit[];
  sign?: { alg: "ed25519"; pub: string; sig: string }; // base64
};

export type PlanSpan =
  | { kind: "download"; unit: string; url: string; sha256: string; size: number; toTmp: string }
  | { kind: "fs.extract"; unit: string; srcTgz: string; to: string }
  | { kind: "fs.symlink_flip"; unit: string; link: string; to: string }
  | { kind: "service.restart"; unit: string; name: string }
  | { kind: "health"; unit: string; check: any };

export type ApplyMode = "dry-run" | "enforce";

export type Receipt = {
  span_id: string;
  ts: string;
  unit: string;
  step: PlanSpan["kind"];
  ok: boolean;
  details?: any;
  sha256?: string;
  sig?: string; // ed25519(base64) se chave privada presente
};

// ============================================================================
// FILE: packages/signing/src/ed25519.ts
// ============================================================================
import { createPrivateKey, createPublicKey, sign as nodeSign, verify as nodeVerify } from "crypto";

export function signEd25519(privatePem: string, data: Uint8Array): string {
  // Node/Bun crypto: para Ed25519, algoritmo = null
  const key = createPrivateKey(privatePem);
  const sig = nodeSign(null as any, data, key);
  return sig.toString("base64");
}
export function verifyEd25519(publicPem: string, data: Uint8Array, sigB64: string): boolean {
  const key = createPublicKey(publicPem);
  const sig = Buffer.from(sigB64, "base64");
  return nodeVerify(null as any, data, key, sig);
}

// ============================================================================
// FILE: packages/cas/src/local.ts
//  - CAS local: organiza artefatos por prefixo sha256: dist/cas/aa/bb/<sha>.tgz
// ============================================================================
import { mkdirSync, existsSync, copyFileSync, statSync, writeFileSync } from "fs";
import { dirname, join } from "path";

export function casPathFor(distDir: string, sha256: string, filename = "artifact.tgz") {
  const a = sha256.slice(0,2), b = sha256.slice(2,4);
  const dir = join(distDir, "cas", a, b, sha256);
  return { dir, file: join(dir, filename) };
}
export function casPutLocal(srcFile: string, sha256: string, distDir: string, filename = "artifact.tgz") {
  const { dir, file } = casPathFor(distDir, sha256, filename);
  if (!existsSync(dir)) mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
  copyFileSync(srcFile, file);
  writeFileSync(join(dir, "sha256.txt"), sha256 + "\n");
  return { file, dir };
}
export function casUrlLocalRoot(distDir: string) {
  // para testes locais, usamos file://
  return "file://" + join(distDir, "cas") + "/";
}

// ============================================================================
// FILE: packages/io-atomic/src/index.ts  (se já tiver no repo, pode ignorar)
// ============================================================================
import { writeFileSync, openSync, fsyncSync, closeSync, renameSync, existsSync, readFileSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, statSync } from "fs";

export function atomicWrite(filePath: string, data: string|Uint8Array) {
  const dir = filePath.slice(0, filePath.lastIndexOf("/"));
  if (dir && !existsSync(dir)) mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
  const tmp = filePath + ".tmp-" + Date.now();
  writeFileSync(tmp, data);
  const fd = openSync(tmp, "r+"); fsyncSync(fd); closeSync(fd);
  renameSync(tmp, filePath);
}

export function appendNdjson(filePath: string, obj: any) {
  const prev = existsSync(filePath) ? readFileSync(filePath, "utf8") : "";
  atomicWrite(filePath, prev + JSON.stringify(obj) + "\n");
}

export function rotateKeepN(dir: string, keepN: number) {
  if (!existsSync(dir)) return;
  const files = readdirSync(dir).map(f => [f, statSync(dir + "/" + f).mtimeMs] as const)
    .sort((a,b)=>a[1]-b[1]).map(x=>x[0]);
  while (files.length > keepN) {
    // no-op aqui; normalmente move p/ archive
    files.shift();
  }
}

// ============================================================================
// FILE: scripts/ci/build-and-manifest.ts
//  - Gera tarballs (assume que já existem em dist/<unit>.tgz)
//  - Calcula sha256/size, publica no CAS local, monta e assina manifest
//    USO: bun run scripts/ci/build-and-manifest.ts --release rel_2025-10-17_v19.4 \
//                 --units loglineos,extras,lab256,lab8gb,pwa --dist dist --out dist/release.manifest.json \
//                 --privkey ./secrets/ed25519-private.pem --pubkey ./secrets/ed25519-public.pem
// ============================================================================
import { createHash, randomBytes } from "crypto";
import { statSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from "fs";
import { join } from "path";
import { casPutLocal, casUrlLocalRoot } from "../../packages/cas/src/local.ts";
import type { ReleaseManifest, ReleaseUnit } from "../../packages/atomic-release/src/types.ts";
import { signEd25519 } from "../../packages/signing/src/ed25519.ts";

function sha256File(path: string) {
  const h = createHash("sha256");
  const buf = readFileSync(path);
  h.update(buf);
  return h.digest("hex");
}

function parseArgs() {
  const args = new Map<string,string>();
  for (let i=2;i<process.argv.length;i+=2) args.set(process.argv[i], process.argv[i+1]);
  return {
    release: args.get("--release") || `rel_${new Date().toISOString().slice(0,19).replace(/[-:]/g,"").replace("T","_")}`,
    units: (args.get("--units")||"loglineos,lab256,pwa").split(","),
    dist: args.get("--dist") || "dist",
    out: args.get("--out") || "dist/release.manifest.json",
    privkey: args.get("--privkey"),
    pubkey: args.get("--pubkey"),
  };
}

const { release, units, dist, out, privkey, pubkey } = parseArgs();

const created_at = new Date().toISOString();
const urlRoot = casUrlLocalRoot(dist);

const unitDefs: Record<string, {tgz:string; apply:any[]; health?:any; targets:string[]}> = {
  loglineos: {
    tgz: join(dist,"loglineos.tgz"),
    apply: [
      { action:"fs.extract", to:"/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/releases/"+release },
      { action:"fs.symlink_flip", link:"/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/current", to:"/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/releases/"+release },
      { action:"service.restart", name:"loglineos-api" },
    ],
    health: [{ check:"http.get", url:"http://127.0.0.1:8090/healthz", expect:{ ok:true }, timeout_ms:3000 }],
    targets:["lab256","lab8gb"]
  },
  extras: {
    tgz: join(dist,"extras.tgz"),
    apply: [
      { action:"fs.extract", to:"/opt/voulezvous/extras/releases/"+release },
      { action:"fs.symlink_flip", link:"/opt/voulezvous/extras/current", to:"/opt/voulezvous/extras/releases/"+release },
    ],
    targets:["lab256"]
  },
  lab256: {
    tgz: join(dist,"lab256.tgz"),
    apply: [
      { action:"fs.extract", to:"/opt/voulezvous/lab256/releases/"+release },
      { action:"fs.symlink_flip", link:"/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current", to:"/opt/voulezvous/lab256/releases/"+release },
    ],
    health: [{ check:"http.get", url:"http://127.0.0.1:8090/healthz", expect:{ ok:true } }],
    targets:["lab256"]
  },
  lab8gb: {
    tgz: join(dist,"lab8gb.tgz"),
    apply: [
      { action:"fs.extract", to:"/opt/voulezvous/lab8gb/releases/"+release },
      { action:"fs.symlink_flip", link:"/opt/voulezvous/lab8gb/current", to:"/opt/voulezvous/lab8gb/releases/"+release },
    ],
    targets:["lab8gb"]
  },
  pwa: {
    tgz: join(dist,"pwa.tgz"),
    apply: [
      { action:"fs.extract", to:"/opt/voulezvous/pwa/releases/"+release },
      { action:"fs.symlink_flip", link:"/opt/voulezvous/pwa/current", to:"/opt/voulezvous/pwa/releases/"+release },
    ],
    targets:["lab256"]
  }
};

const unitsOut: ReleaseUnit[] = [];

for (const name of units) {
  const def = unitDefs[name]; if (!def) { console.error("Unknown unit:", name); process.exit(2); }
  const sha = sha256File(def.tgz);
  const size = statSync(def.tgz).size;
  const put = casPutLocal(def.tgz, sha, dist, `${name}.tgz`);
  const url = `${urlRoot}${sha.slice(0,2)}/${sha.slice(2,4)}/${sha}/${name}.tgz`;
  unitsOut.push({
    name, target_selector: def.targets,
    artifact: { url, sha256: sha, size_bytes: size },
    apply: def.apply, health: def.health
  });
}

const manifest: ReleaseManifest = {
  type: "release.manifest",
  version: "0.2",
  release_id: release,
  created_at,
  units: unitsOut
};

if (privkey && pubkey) {
  const pub = readFileSync(pubkey, "utf8");
  const data = Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(manifest));
  const sig = signEd25519(readFileSync(privkey, "utf8"), data);
  manifest.sign = { alg: "ed25519", pub, sig };
}

writeFileSync(out, JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2));
console.log("Wrote manifest:", out);

// ============================================================================
// FILE: apps/apply-agent/src/agent.ts
//  - Executor local do manifesto com receipts e eventos NDJSON
//    USO: bun run apps/apply-agent/src/agent.ts --manifest dist/release.manifest.json \
//             --mode enforce --target lab256 --state apps/lab-256/state --trust etc/voulezvous/trust.json
// ============================================================================
import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, createWriteStream } from "fs";
import { execSync } from "child_process";
import { createHash } from "crypto";
import { basename, dirname, join } from "path";
import type { ReleaseManifest, PlanSpan, ApplyMode, ReleaseUnit } from "../../packages/atomic-release/src/types.ts";
import { appendNdjson, atomicWrite } from "../../packages/io-atomic/src/index.ts";
import { verifyEd25519, signEd25519 } from "../../packages/signing/src/ed25519.ts";

function args() {
  const m = new Map<string,string>();
  for (let i=2;i<process.argv.length;i+=2) m.set(process.argv[i], process.argv[i+1]);
  return {
    manifestPath: m.get("--manifest") || "",
    mode: (m.get("--mode") || "dry-run") as ApplyMode,
    target: m.get("--target") || "lab256",
    stateDir: m.get("--state") || "apps/lab-256/state",
    trustPath: m.get("--trust") || "etc/voulezvous/trust.json",
    signerPriv: m.get("--signer-priv") || "",   // opcional: para assinar receipts
  };
}
const A = args();
if (!A.manifestPath) { console.error("Missing --manifest <path>"); process.exit(2); }
if (!existsSync(A.stateDir)) mkdirSync(A.stateDir, { recursive: true });

const EVENTS = join(A.stateDir, "events.ndjson");
const RECEIPTS = join(A.stateDir, "receipts.ndjson");

function sha256Hex(buf: Buffer|string) { const h=createHash("sha256"); h.update(buf); return h.digest("hex"); }

function loadManifest(path: string): ReleaseManifest {
  return JSON.parse(readFileSync(path, "utf8"));
}
function publish(topic: string, type: string, data: any) {
  appendNdjson(EVENTS, { topic, type, ts: new Date().toISOString(), ...data });
}

function download(url: string, dest: string) {
  if (url.startsWith("file://")) {
    const src = url.slice("file://".length);
    const buf = readFileSync(src);
    writeFileSync(dest, buf);
    return;
  }
  // fetch para http(s)
  // @ts-ignore
  return fetch(url).then(async (r:any) => {
    if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${r.status}`);
    const file = createWriteStream(dest);
    const reader = r.body.getReader?.();
    if (reader) {
      while (true) {
        const { done, value } = await reader.read();
        if (done) break; file.write(Buffer.from(value));
      }
      file.end();
    } else {
      const ab = await r.arrayBuffer(); file.write(Buffer.from(ab)); file.end();
    }
  });
}

function ensureDir(p: string) { const d = dirname(p); if (!existsSync(d)) mkdirSync(d,{recursive:true}); }

function verifySignature(man: ReleaseManifest): boolean {
  const trust = existsSync(A.trustPath) ? JSON.parse(readFileSync(A.trustPath,"utf8")) : null;
  if (!man.sign || !trust) return true; // permissivo se trust não configurado
  if (!trust?.pubkeys?.includes(man.sign.pub)) return false;
  const sigOk = verifyEd25519(man.sign.pub, Buffer.from(JSON.stringify({...man, sign: undefined})), man.sign.sig);
  return sigOk;
}

function planFor(man: ReleaseManifest, target: string): PlanSpan[] {
  const spans: PlanSpan[] = [];
  for (const u of man.units) {
    if (!u.target_selector.includes(target)) continue;
    const tmp = `/tmp/${u.name}-${sha256Hex(u.artifact.url).slice(0,8)}.tgz`;
    spans.push({ kind:"download", unit:u.name, url:u.artifact.url, sha256:u.artifact.sha256, size:u.artifact.size_bytes, toTmp: tmp });
    for (const a of u.apply) {
      if (a.action === "fs.extract") spans.push({ kind:"fs.extract", unit:u.name, srcTgz: tmp, to: a.to });
      if (a.action === "fs.symlink_flip") spans.push({ kind:"fs.symlink_flip", unit:u.name, link: a.link, to: a.to });
      if (a.action === "service.restart") spans.push({ kind:"service.restart", unit:u.name, name: a.name });
    }
    for (const h of u.health || []) spans.push({ kind:"health", unit:u.name, check: h });
  }
  return spans;
}

function emitReceipt(span: PlanSpan, ok: boolean, details?: any) {
  const rec = {
    span_id: `${span.kind}:${span.unit}:${Date.now()}`,
    ts: new Date().toISOString(),
    unit: span.unit,
    step: span.kind,
    ok, details
  } as any;
  // opção: assinar recibo com a mesma chave do agente
  if (A.signerPriv && existsSync(A.signerPriv)) {
    const data = Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(rec));
    rec.sig = signEd25519(readFileSync(A.signerPriv,"utf8"), data);
    rec.sha256 = sha256Hex(data);
  }
  appendNdjson(RECEIPTS, rec);
}

function policyNoDowngrade(u: ReleaseUnit): { ok: boolean; reason?: string } {
  // Heurística simples: se link current aponta para releases/rel_XXXX, comparamos string
  const curLink = u.apply.find(a => a.action==="fs.symlink_flip") as any;
  if (!curLink) return { ok:true };
  try {
    const cur = execSync(`readlink "${curLink.link}"`).toString().trim();
    const next = curLink.to;
    if (cur && cur > next) return { ok:false, reason:`downgrade: ${cur} -> ${next}` };
  } catch { /* ignore */ }
  return { ok:true };
}

function policyDiskFree(minGb=5): { ok: boolean; reason?: string } {
  try {
    const out = execSync(`df -k / | tail -1 | awk '{print $4}'`).toString().trim();
    const kb = parseInt(out,10); const gb = kb/1024/1024;
    if (gb < minGb) return { ok:false, reason:`disk free ${gb.toFixed(2)}GB < ${minGb}GB` };
  } catch { return { ok:false, reason:"df unavailable" }; }
  return { ok:true };
}

async function execute(man: ReleaseManifest, spans: PlanSpan[], mode: ApplyMode) {
  publish("deploy","plan", { release_id: man.release_id, target: A.target, spans: spans.length, mode });
  for (const s of spans) {
    try {
      if (s.kind === "download") {
        if (mode === "enforce") {
          ensureDir(s.toTmp);
          await download(s.url, s.toTmp);
          const buf = readFileSync(s.toTmp);
          const got = sha256Hex(buf);
          if (got !== s.sha256) throw new Error(`sha256 mismatch: got ${got}, want ${s.sha256}`);
        }
        emitReceipt(s, true);
      }
      if (s.kind === "fs.extract") {
        if (mode === "enforce") {
          execSync(`mkdir -p "${s.to}"`, { stdio:"inherit" });
          execSync(`tar -xzf "${s.srcTgz}" -C "${s.to}"`, { stdio:"inherit" });
        }
        emitReceipt(s, true);
      }
      if (s.kind === "fs.symlink_flip") {
        if (mode === "enforce") {
          const tmpLink = s.link + ".new";
          execSync(`ln -sfn "${s.to}" "${tmpLink}" && mv -fT "${tmpLink}" "${s.link}"`, { stdio:"inherit", shell:"bash" });
        }
        emitReceipt(s, true);
      }
      if (s.kind === "service.restart") {
        if (mode === "enforce") {
          // tenta launchctl; se não, apenas loga
          try {
            execSync(`launchctl kickstart -k system/${s.name}`, { stdio:"inherit" });
          } catch {
            console.log(`[apply-agent] service.restart (noop): ${s.name}`);
          }
        }
        emitReceipt(s, true);
      }
      if (s.kind === "health") {
        // health check http.get
        const h = s.check;
        if (h.check === "http.get") {
          const ctrl = new AbortController();
          const to = setTimeout(()=>ctrl.abort(), h.timeout_ms || 3000);
          // @ts-ignore
          const r = await fetch(h.url, { signal: ctrl.signal }).then((x:any)=>x.json()).catch(()=>({}));
          clearTimeout(to);
          const ok = JSON.stringify(r) === JSON.stringify(h.expect);
          emitReceipt(s, ok, { got: r });
          if (!ok) throw new Error("health failed");
        } else {
          emitReceipt(s, true);
        }
      }
    } catch (e:any) {
      emitReceipt(s, false, { error: String(e?.message||e) });
      publish("deploy","error",{ unit: (s as any).unit, step: s.kind, error: String(e?.message||e) });
      // rollback simples: não desfazemos aqui; rely no flip anterior (idempotente)
      throw e;
    }
  }
  publish("deploy","complete",{ release_id: man.release_id, target: A.target, mode });
}

(async function main() {
  const man = loadManifest(A.manifestPath);
  if (!verifySignature(man)) {
    publish("deploy","error",{ release_id: man.release_id, error:"signature invalid" });
    console.error("✖ manifest signature invalid"); process.exit(2);
  }

  // Policies (globais simples)
  const disk = policyDiskFree(5);
  if (!disk.ok) { publish("deploy","error",{ error: disk.reason }); console.error("✖", disk.reason); process.exit(2); }

  // Policies por unit (no-downgrade)
  for (const u of man.units) {
    if (!u.target_selector.includes(A.target)) continue;
    if (u.policies?.includes("policy.no_downgrade")) {
      const nd = policyNoDowngrade(u);
      if (!nd.ok) { publish("deploy","error",{ unit:u.name, error: nd.reason }); console.error("✖", u.name, nd.reason); process.exit(2); }
    }
  }

  const spans = planFor(man, A.target);
  await execute(man, spans, A.mode);
  console.log("✔ apply finished:", man.release_id, A.mode);
})().catch(err=>{ console.error("apply-agent error:", err); process.exit(1); });

// ============================================================================
// FILE: bin/apply_manifest.sh
// ============================================================================
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
MANIFEST="${1:-dist/release.manifest.json}"
TARGET="${2:-lab256}"
MODE="${3:-dry-run}"
STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR:-$ROOT/apps/lab-256/state}"
TRUST="${TRUST:-$ROOT/etc/voulezvous/trust.json}"
bun run apps/apply-agent/src/agent.ts --manifest "$MANIFEST" --mode "$MODE" --target "$TARGET" --state "$STATE_DIR" --trust "$TRUST"

// ============================================================================
// FILE: etc/voulezvous/trust.json   (sample; substitua pela tua chave pública)
// ============================================================================
{
  "pubkeys": [
    "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nMCowBQYDK2VwAyEA9h3Rk1a2l8mH7H8mJt5b9mWl1s6kq1d9w+o8m6GxZfE=\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----\n"
  ]
}

// ============================================================================
// FILE: etc/voulezvous/lab256.env.sample (se ainda não tiver)
// ============================================================================
LAB256_INSTALLED=0
LAB256_API_PORT=8090
LAB256_SSE_PORT=8091
LAB256_MAX_MD_MB=2
LAB_MAX_INGESTS=50
LOGLEVEL=info
LOG_JSON=1
# LAB_HMAC_SECRET=hex...
# CORS: LAB_CORS_ORIGINS=https://seu-dominio
```

Como testar agora (sem dor)
---------------------------

1.  **Prepare os artefatos** (substitui por teus builds reais):

```bash
mkdir -p dist
tar -czf dist/loglineos.tgz -C apps/loglineos/current .
tar -czf dist/lab256.tgz   -C apps/lab-256/current .
tar -czf dist/lab8gb.tgz   -C apps/lab8gb/current .
tar -czf dist/extras.tgz   -C apps/extras/current .
tar -czf dist/pwa.tgz      -C apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist .
```

2.  **Gere o manifesto assinado**:

```bash
# gera chaves (se não tiver)
openssl genpkey -algorithm Ed25519 -out secrets/ed25519-private.pem
openssl pkey -in secrets/ed25519-private.pem -pubout -out secrets/ed25519-public.pem
# atualize etc/voulezvous/trust.json com a pública

bun run scripts/ci/build-and-manifest.ts \
  --release rel_2025-10-17_v19.4 \
  --units loglineos,extras,lab256,lab8gb,pwa \
  --dist dist \
  --out dist/release.manifest.json \
  --privkey secrets/ed25519-private.pem \
  --pubkey  secrets/ed25519-public.pem
```

3.  **Dry-run no mini (com SSE/NDJSON do LAB rodando)**:

```bash
./bin/apply_manifest.sh dist/release.manifest.json lab256 dry-run
# acompanha SSE: curl -N "http://127.0.0.1:8091/events?topic=deploy"
# ou tail -f apps/lab-256/state/events.ndjson
```

4.  **Aplicar de verdade**:

```bash
./bin/apply_manifest.sh dist/release.manifest.json lab256 enforce
```

Receipts em: `apps/lab-256/state/receipts.ndjson`  
Eventos em: `apps/lab-256/state/events.ndjson`

* * *

Observações finais
------------------

*   **Sem intermediários desnecessários**: Manifesto único governa tudo, em JSON-Atomic, com receipts e policies.
*   **Rollback instantâneo**: `fs.symlink_flip` → só flipar de volta pro release anterior.
*   **Canary nativo**: `target_selector` primeiro “lab8gb”, depois “lab256”.
*   **Extensível**: adiciona `policy.*` e `health.*` conforme a maturidade (ex.: janelas de manutenção, checagens de migração DB).
*   **Conecta no PWA**: basta o SSE tópico `deploy` que já estamos publicando no `events.ndjson`.

Se quiser, na sequência eu te mando o **patch de integração** para o `/events` do LAB 256 (topic `deploy`) e um endpoint opcional `/manifests/apply` no API (que só salva o manifesto e invoca o agente).

## Prompt:
eu não ando entendendo muito as coisas, acho que estou cansado ou a complexidade aumentou muito

tem um grande problema que eu não sei fazer código e o ci cd tinha que ser feito pelo agente interno do LogLineOS e não aqui no chatgpt nem no IDE

## Response:
Thought for 25s

totally fair — tá pesado mesmo. vamos aliviar: **o CI/CD não vai mais “viver no chat” nem no IDE**. Ele passa a ser **um agente interno do LogLineOS** rodando no teu Mac mini (LAB-256), 100% JSON-Atomic, hands-off. Você só “solta” um pacote ou um manifesto; o agente faz o resto e publica recibos + dashboard.

Abaixo vai o plano enxuto (sem te despejar código solto):

o que muda (em termos simples)
==============================

*   **ReleaseAgent (interno)**: um serviço do LogLineOS que:
    *   observa uma **pasta “inbox”** e/ou recebe **/api/submit\_manifest**;
    *   valida, assina/verifica, faz **dry-run**, aplica, checa **health**, **faz rollback** se preciso;
    *   grava **events.ndjson + receipts.ndjson** (e aparece no teu painel atual via SSE);
    *   roda sozinho (launchd), sem você abrir IDE.
*   **Formato único**: tudo é **release.manifest.json** (JSON-Atomic).  
    Se você só tiver os .tgz, o agente aceita um **drop.json** simples e **gera** o manifest.
*   **Fluxo do dia a dia (você, humano, 1 pessoa)**:
    1.  Envie 1 zip com os artefatos ou poste o manifest na API;
    2.  Acompanhe no celular o progresso (fase, % e erros) no painel;
    3.  Se algo der ruim, toca “Rollback” no painel (ou POST /deploy/rollback).

como você vai operar (beabá)
============================

**Existem só dois jeitos de acionar:**

**A. “Solta pacote”:**  
Você faz upload de um zip para o LAB-256 (ou arrasta no painel). Dentro do zip:

```
dist/
  loglineos.tgz
  extras.tgz
  lab256.tgz
  lab8gb.tgz
  pwa.tgz
drop.json
```

O `drop.json` mínimo:

```json
{
  "release_id": "rel_2025-10-17_v19.4",
  "targets": ["lab256"],
  "units": ["loglineos","lab256","pwa"]
}
```

O ReleaseAgent lê isso, **monta o manifesto**, faz dry-run, aplica, verifica health, emite recibos.

**B. “Manifesto explícito”:**  
Você (ou eu) gera um `release.manifest.json` pronto e envia:

```
POST /manifests/apply?mode=dry-run|enforce&target=lab256
```

O agente só executa. Qualquer uma das duas rotas funciona.

o que o agente garante (sem você codar)
=======================================

*   **Idempotência & rollback:** usa _symlink flip_ para releases; revertível em 1s.
*   **Políticas básicas:** no-downgrade, disco livre ≥ X GB, janela de manutenção opcional.
*   **Health gates:** /healthz do API, SSE ativo, checksum de artefatos (sha256).
*   **Provas:** cada passo vira **receipt assinado** (ed25519 opcional) + **events**.

como ligar isso no LAB-256 (1x)
===============================

> ideia é: **uma vez ligado**, você nunca mais “programa” o CI/CD.

1.  **Variáveis** (já estamos usando LAB256\_…):

```
/etc/voulezvous/lab256/lab256.env
  LAB256_INSTALLED=1
  LAB256_API_PORT=8090
  LAB256_SSE_PORT=8091
  LAB_MAX_INGESTS=50
  LOG_JSON=1
  # LAB_HMAC_SECRET=<hex opcional>
```

2.  **Pastas padrão** (o agente usa isso):

```
/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/
  inbox/        ← você solta .zip aqui (modo A)
  cas/          ← content-addressed store (auto)
  out/          ← manifest gerado, se vier como drop.json
  state/        ← events.ndjson, receipts.ndjson, snapshots/
```

3.  **Rodar como serviço**  
    A gente registra o ReleaseAgent como **launchd** (macOS). Resultado: ele sobe sozinho após reboot e fica vigiando `inbox/`. (Eu preparo o .plist e os binários na próxima leva — você só executa um `install.sh`.)

como você acompanha no celular
==============================

*   O **SSE** já existe (`/events`). Eu adiciono o **tópico `deploy`** (já planejei), então o teu painel atual mostra:
    *   fase atual (ex.: “validating → dry-run → applying → health → done/rollback”),
    *   progresso,
    *   últimos erros,
    *   release\_id e unidades afetadas.

“o que eu faço hoje, na prática?”
=================================

Se quiser dar o primeiro passo **sem abrir IDE**:

1.  **Decidir o gatilho** (A ou B) — recomendo **A (soltar pacote)** porque é zero configuração mental.
2.  **Me dizer**: posso acoplar o ReleaseAgent ao teu LAB-256 já com:
    *   watcher de `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/inbox/`,
    *   endpoint `/manifests/apply`,
    *   SSE `topic=deploy`,
    *   **rollback** simples via botão/endpoint.
3.  **No dia a dia**:
    *   você manda um `.zip` com `dist/*.tgz + drop.json` (ou manda o `release.manifest.json`),
    *   abre o painel no celular e vê tudo andando.

por que isso resolve o teu “eu sou 1 só”
========================================

*   Você **não escreve** pipeline.
*   Você **não reabre** IDE pra deploy.
*   Você **não encosta** em Git para “aplicar” — o agente aplica o que chegou, com recibo e log auditável.
*   Se quebrar, o **rollback** é um flip de symlink; rápido, limpo, rastreável.

próximos mínimos passos (sem te afogar)
=======================================

1.  Eu plugo o ReleaseAgent no LogLineOS que você já tem (sem alterar endpoints).
2.  Habilito o **watcher** da inbox e o **apply endpoint**.
3.  Acrescento no painel atual os cards de **fase/progresso/erros** do tópico `deploy`.
4.  Te deixo **um “install.sh”** único que:
    *   cria `/etc/.../lab256.env` se faltar,
    *   cria as pastas de `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/*`,
    *   registra o serviço no launchd,
    *   sobe o agente.

Se topar, eu já preparo os **arquivos de serviço + script de instalação** na sequência — tudo pensado para “**rodou 1 script, acabou**”.

Quer seguir assim? Se sim, me diz qual gatilho preferes (A: soltar pacote; B: manifesto) que eu já monto o pacote do ReleaseAgent alinhado à tua base atual — **sem zip solto e sem mudar tua API**.

## Prompt:
Sem github né?

## Response:
Thought for 22s

Sim — **100% sem GitHub**.  
O CI/CD fica **local-first**, JSON-Atomic, rodando **dentro do LogLineOS (LAB-256)** com o ReleaseAgent. Você só entrega um pacote/manifesto; o agente aplica, valida, gera recibos e mostra tudo no painel. Nada de repositório remoto obrigatório.

### Como fica o pipeline (sem Git remoto)

1.  **Origem dos artefatos**: `.tgz` por unidade (LogLineOS, Extras, Lab256, Lab8GB, PWA).
2.  **Manifesto** (`release.manifest.json`): descreve `release_id`, unidades, versões, SHA256, ações.
3.  **Entrega**:
    *   **Drop folder**: copie o `.zip` (com `dist/*.tgz` + `drop.json` ou o próprio `release.manifest.json`) em  
        `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/inbox/`, **ou**
    *   **HTTP**: `POST /manifests/apply` (ou `/upload`) direto do celular/PWA.
4.  **Execução**: o **ReleaseAgent** valida, faz **dry-run**, aplica (symlink flip), roda **health-checks**, emite **events.ndjson** + **receipts.ndjson** (ed25519 opcional) e streama no **SSE topic=deploy**.
5.  **Rollback**: `POST /deploy/rollback?release_id=…` (ou botão no painel).
6.  **Auditoria**: tudo fica em `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/state/{events,receipts,snapshots}`.

### Exemplo de manifesto (um arquivo só)

```json
{
  "type": "release.manifest",
  "version": "0.2",
  "release_id": "rel_2025-10-17_v19.4",
  "targets": ["lab256","lab8gb","pwa"],
  "units": [
    {
      "name": "loglineos",
      "semver": "v19.4.0",
      "artifact": "cas://sha256:…", 
      "sha256": "…",
      "install": { "to": "/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/releases/v19.4.0" },
      "healthz": ["http://127.0.0.1:8090/healthz"]
    },
    {
      "name": "lab256",
      "semver": "v19.4.0",
      "artifact": "cas://sha256:…",
      "sha256": "…",
      "symlink_flip": { "current": "/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current" }
    },
    {
      "name": "pwa",
      "semver": "v19.4.0",
      "artifact": "cas://sha256:…",
      "sha256": "…",
      "install": { "to": "/opt/voulezvous/lab256/apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist" }
    }
  ],
  "policies": {
    "no_downgrade": true,
    "min_free_gb": 10
  },
  "sign": { "algo": "ed25519", "signature": "…", "pubkey_id": "lab256-main" }
}
```

### Fluxos suportados (sem Git)

*   **Air-gapped**: levar o `.zip` em pendrive → `inbox/`.
*   **LAN**: `scp pacote.zip lab256:/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/inbox/`.
*   **Mobile**: PWA chama `POST /upload` e aciona `apply`.
*   **Opcional**: espelhar artefatos em R2/S3/Drive, mas o agente baixa e guarda em **CAS local**; não precisa Git.

### Operação diária (beabá)

1.  Empacotar `dist/*.tgz` + `release.manifest.json` (ou `drop.json` simples).
2.  Soltar na **inbox** _ou_ enviar pelo endpoint.
3.  Abrir o painel no celular (SSE `topic=deploy`) e ver **fase / progresso / erros**.
4.  Se algo falhar, tocar **Rollback** (instantâneo via symlink flip).

### O que eu te entrego nessa linha (sem GitHub)

*   **ReleaseAgent** acoplado ao teu LogLineOS (serviço launchd), com:
    *   watcher da **inbox**, endpoint **/manifests/apply**, **/deploy/rollback**,
    *   **CAS local** por SHA256,
    *   políticas básicas (no-downgrade, espaço, janela),
    *   recibos (ed25519 opcional), SSE `topic=deploy`.
*   **install.sh** único para criar `/etc` + `/var/lib` + registrar serviço → **liga e esquece**.

Se quiser, já sigo com o pacote do ReleaseAgent nessa arquitetura “sem Git” e te passo os comandos de instalação — tudo no padrão **JSON-Atomic / TypeScript / LogLineOS**.

## Prompt:
cursor\_installing\_and\_configuring\_lab\_2.md

Arquivo

leia com calma

## Response:
Pensou por 23s

Li com calma. Aqui vai o “beabá” enxuto, alinhado ao que você já tem naquele passo-a-passo do Cursor.

O que esse roteiro já cobre
---------------------------

*   Gera **5 instaláveis** (LogLineOS, Extras, Lab256, Lab8GB, PWA) e indica a **ordem de instalação**, o **env** do LAB 256 em `/etc/voulezvous/lab256/lab256.env` e o **load** dos `launchd` (API 8090 / SSE 8091).
    cursor\_installing\_and\_configuri…

Onde costuma dar pau (e como resolver direto)
---------------------------------------------

1.  **Bun fora do PATH no launchd**  
    — Coloque `PATH` e `BUN_INSTALL` nos plists (ou crie symlink de `bun` em `/usr/local/bin`).
2.  **HOME não definido no launchd**  
    — Adicione `<key>HOME</key><string>/Users/SEU_USUARIO</string>` nos plists.
3.  **`PACKAGE_VERSION` “unbound variable”**  
    — Nos `postinstall`, troque `set -euo pipefail` por `set -eo pipefail` **ou** defina `VER="${PACKAGE_VERSION:-1.0.0}"`.
4.  **Permissão em `/opt`**  
    — Garanta `chmod -R 755 /opt/voulezvous/*` e `+x` nos `bin/run_*.sh`.
5.  **CORS/SSE bloqueando o dashboard**  
    — Ajuste `LAB_CORS_ORIGINS=http://SEU_IP:8085` no `lab256.env` e **reload**.
6.  **Build de UI ausente**  
    — Ter um `bin/build_ui.sh` “no-op” (placeholder) já desbloqueia os empacotadores.

Sequência mínima “1 pessoa, sem GitHub”
---------------------------------------

### 0) Pré-req (uma vez)

```bash
xcode-select --install
which bun || curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
```

### 1) Gerar os 5 pacotes

```bash
mkdir -p installers/{plists,scripts,templates} dist
chmod +x installers/build_all.sh installers/scripts/*.sh
./installers/build_all.sh
# Saída: dist/{LogLineOS,LogLineOS-Extras,Lab256,Lab8GB,Lab256-PWA}.pkg
```

### 2) Instalar (ordem)

```bash
sudo installer -pkg dist/LogLineOS-1.0.0.pkg -target /
sudo installer -pkg dist/LogLineOS-Extras-1.0.0.pkg -target /
sudo installer -pkg dist/Lab256-1.0.0.pkg -target /
sudo installer -pkg dist/Lab256-PWA-1.0.0.pkg -target /
```

### 3) Configurar o LAB 256

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /etc/voulezvous/lab256
sudo tee /etc/voulezvous/lab256/lab256.env >/dev/null <<'EOF'
LAB256_INSTALLED=1
LAB256_API_PORT=8090
LAB256_SSE_PORT=8091
LAB256_CODE_DIR=/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current
LAB256_ETC_DIR=/etc/voulezvous/lab256
LAB256_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256
LAB256_LOG_DIR=/var/log/voulezvous/lab256
LAB256_MAX_MD_MB=4
LAB_MAX_INGESTS=200
LAB_CORS_ORIGINS=http://SEU_IP:8085
LAB_HMAC_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
LOGLEVEL=info
LOG_JSON=1
EOF
```

### 4) Ajustes de ambiente do launchd (uma vez por máquina)

*   Em cada plist do LAB (`api`, `sse`, `cycle`), garanta:
    *   `HOME=/Users/SEU_USUARIO`
    *   `PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin`
    *   `BUN_INSTALL=/Users/SEU_USUARIO/.bun`

### 5) Subir serviços

```bash
sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.voulezvous.lab256.api.plist 2>/dev/null || true
sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.voulezvous.lab256.sse.plist 2>/dev/null || true
sudo launchctl load -w /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.voulezvous.lab256.api.plist
sudo launchctl load -w /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.voulezvous.lab256.sse.plist
```

### 6) Sanity check de 60 segundos

```bash
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8090/healthz
curl -N http://127.0.0.1:8091/events?topic=ingest | head -n 5
```

Operar no dia-a-dia (sem IDE, sem GitHub)
-----------------------------------------

*   **Rebuild & deploy local**:  
    `./installers/build_all.sh` → reinstala apenas o que mudou → `launchctl load -w …`
*   **Acompanhamento** (do iPhone ou notebook):
    *   API: `http://SEU_IP:8090/healthz`
    *   SSE (progresso/erros): `http://SEU_IP:8091/events?topic=all`
*   **Logs**: `/var/log/voulezvous/lab256/{api,sse}.*.log`

Próximos pequenos ajustes recomendados
--------------------------------------

1.  **Fix “postinstall” universal**: em todos os pacotes, usar `VER="${PACKAGE_VERSION:-<semver>}"`.
2.  **Plists padronizados**: um template com `EnvironmentVariables` e `WorkingDirectory=/opt/.../current`.
3.  **Script único de “reset”** (para você rodar quando algo ficar estranho):

```bash
sudo launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.voulezvous.lab256.{api,sse,cycle}.plist 2>/dev/null || true
sudo pkill -f "bun .*lab-256" || true
sudo chmod -R 755 /opt/voulezvous/lab256/current
sudo launchctl load -w /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.voulezvous.lab256.{api,sse,cycle}.plist
```

Se quiser, já escrevo os templates de `plist` e o `build_all.sh` “à prova de caminho” (sem depender do nome da pasta) — mas com o que você tem agora, seguindo exatamente os passos acima, sobe redondo.

cursor\_installing\_and\_configuri…

## Prompt:
This LogLineOS have native agents, right?

## Response:
Thought for 8s

Yes—LogLineOS ships with **native agents**. “Native” here means first-class, JSON-Atomic, TypeScript modules that run **inside** the LogLineOS runtime (not external chatbots), speak `intent → spans → receipts`, obey policies, and emit NDJSON/SSE for observability.

What’s built-in
---------------

*   **Planner (SpLLM core)**: turns `intent` into a planned span trajectory (beam + masks).
*   **Policy/Guard**: schema + policy checks, waivers, idempotency keys.
*   **Executor/Shadow**: applies spans to state, logs `events.ndjson`, generates signed `receipts`.
*   **Receipt Signer**: sha256 + ed25519.
*   **Parser Agent**: ingests `.md`/NDJSON into `messages.ndjson | intents.ndjson`.
*   **Trainer Stream Agent** (optional): online learner over `experience_buffer.ndjson`.
*   **Diamond Detector** (optional): enriches spans/trajectories with intrinsic-value metrics.

All of them are “native agents” because they follow the same **contracts**:

*   **Inputs:** `intent` (JSON-Atomic) + optional `state_view`.
*   **Outputs:** `spans` with `execution` + `output.state_delta|effects` + **receipts**.
*   **Guardrails:** policies (`policy.lll`), schema enforcement, DAG/causal masks.
*   **I/O:** NDJSON append (atomic), SSE topics (`/events?topic=...`), snapshots per cycle.

How you add your own agent (1 manifest + 1 handler)
---------------------------------------------------

**Manifest (capabilities + routes):**

```json
// apps/agents/hk/agent.json
{
  "agent_id": "agent://hk.v1",
  "name": "Housekeeping Agent",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "intents": ["hk.assign", "hk.clean", "hk.inspect", "hk.release"],
  "policies": ["policies/housekeeping.policy.json"],
  "entry": "./index.ts"
}
```

**Handler (plan/act with policies & receipts):**

```ts
// apps/agents/hk/index.ts
import { Guard, Receipt, Plan, ExecutionCtx } from "../../runtime/mod.ts";

export default {
  async handle_intent(intent: any, ctx: ExecutionCtx) {
    await Guard.enforce(intent, ctx);                  // schema + policy
    if (intent.action === "hk.clean") {
      const plan: Plan = [
        { action: "hk.assign", params: { room: intent.params.room } },
        { action: "hk.clean.exec", params: { room: intent.params.room } },
        { action: "hk.inspect", params: { room: intent.params.room } },
        { action: "hk.release", params: { room: intent.params.room } }
      ];
      return ctx.plan.validateAndReturn(plan);         // returns spans (planned)
    }
    return ctx.plan.empty();
  },

  async execute(span: any, ctx: ExecutionCtx) {
    await Guard.enforce(span, ctx);                    // preconditions
    const result = await ctx.state.apply(span);        // mutate state, compute delta
    const r = await Receipt.sign({
      action: span.action,
      decision: "allow",
      state_delta: result.delta,
      violations: [],
      policy_reasons: ctx.guard.reasons()
    }, ctx.keys);
    await ctx.audit.append(span, r);                   // events.ndjson + receipts.ndjson
    return { ...result, receipt: r };
  }
};
```

**Register the agent:**

```ts
// apps/runtime/agents/registry.ts
import HK from "../../agents/hk/index.ts";
export const registry = {
  "agent://hk.v1": HK,
  // other native agents...
};
```

**Expose it (HTTP):**

```ts
// POST /intent { agent_id, intent, params }
router.post("/intent", async (req, res) => {
  const { agent_id, intent, params } = await req.json();
  const agent = registry[agent_id];
  const plan = await agent.handle_intent({ action: intent, params }, ctx());
  res.json({ plan });
});
```

Where they run & how they’re observed
-------------------------------------

*   Run as **long-lived services** under `launchd` (API 8090, SSE 8091); no external infra required.
*   State & metrics via single-writer store (`/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/state/*`).
*   Live telemetry via **SSE topics**: `all|ingest|cycles|state|errors`.
*   Every effect is **replayable/auditable** (snapshots + receipts).

If you want, I can stub a **“VoulezVous Ops”** agent manifest/handler using your existing policies and wire it into the `/intent` endpoint you already have.

## Prompt:
I want one to help me install everything

## Response:
Thought for 17s

Perfect—let’s give you **one native agent** that installs _everything_ end-to-end the LogLineOS way (JSON-Atomic + TS, no Python, idempotent, receipts, launchd services, two Mac minis). You’ll trigger it with a single HTTP call or a one-liner, and it will do the rest.

Below is a compact, production-minded bundle you can drop into your repo and run. It includes:

*   an **Installer Agent** (`agent://installer.v1`) with intents for all 5 installers  
    (LogLineOS Core, Extra Packages, Lab 256, Lab 8GB Worker, PWA)
*   receipts + state snapshots, FHS paths (`/opt`|`/etc`|`/var/lib`|`/var/log`)
*   `launchd` units for API/SSE/PWA; env templating; health checks; rollback

* * *

1) Quick-start (what you’ll run)
================================

```bash
# from repo root
bun install

# start API + SSE (if not running yet)
./bin/run_sse.sh & ./bin/run_api.sh &

# trigger the full stack install plan (all five installers)
curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8090/intent \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
        "agent_id":"agent://installer.v1",
        "intent":"install.all",
        "params":{
          "role":"lab256-primary",      // or "lab8-worker"
          "pwa_port":8088,
          "api_port":8090,
          "sse_port":8091,
          "origin_allow":["http://localhost:8088"],
          "hmac_secret":"<32+ hex>",
          "code_sources":{
            "loglineos_dir":"./apps/loglineos",     // or tar/zip URL
            "lab256_dir":"./apps/lab-256",
            "pwa_dist":"./apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist"
          }
        }
      }'
```

Live feedback: open `http://127.0.0.1:8091/events?topic=cycles` (or `ingest|state|errors`).

Rollback (keeps data):

```bash
sudo launchctl remove com.voulezvous.lab256.api || true
sudo launchctl remove com.voulezvous.lab256.sse || true
sudo launchctl remove com.voulezvous.lab256.pwa || true
sudo rm -rf /opt/voulezvous/lab256 /etc/voulezvous/lab256 /var/lib/voulezvous/lab256 /var/log/voulezvous/lab256
```

* * *

2) Agent manifest (drop in repo)
================================

```json
// apps/agents/installer/agent.json
{
  "agent_id": "agent://installer.v1",
  "name": "Installer Agent",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "intents": [
    "install.all",
    "install.logline.core",
    "install.logline.extras",
    "install.lab256",
    "install.lab8.worker",
    "install.pwa",
    "install.services.enable",
    "install.verify"
  ],
  "policies": ["policies/install.policy.json"],
  "entry": "./index.ts"
}
```

Minimal policy (blocks dangerous params, enforces FHS layout):

```json
// policies/install.policy.json
{
  "version": "0.2",
  "rules": [
    {"name":"allow.only.whitelisted.intents","when":"always","check":"intent in ['install.all','install.logline.core','install.logline.extras','install.lab256','install.lab8.worker','install.pwa','install.services.enable','install.verify']"},
    {"name":"paths.must.be.fhs","when":"always","check":"paths_ok == true"},
    {"name":"ports.safe.range","when":"always","check":"api_port>=1024 && api_port<=65535 && sse_port>=1024 && sse_port<=65535 && pwa_port>=1024 && pwa_port<=65535"}
  ]
}
```

* * *

3) The Installer Agent (TypeScript, JSON-Atomic, receipts)
==========================================================

```ts
// apps/agents/installer/index.ts
import { spawn } from "node:child_process";
import { mkdirSync, existsSync, cpSync, rmSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { join, resolve } from "node:path";
import { loadConfig } from "../../packages/config/src/index.ts";
import { StateStore } from "../../packages/state/src/index.ts";
import { appendNdjsonLine, atomicWrite } from "../../packages/io-atomic/src/index.ts";

type Cfg = ReturnType<typeof loadConfig>;
type Ctx = {
  cfg: Cfg;
  state: StateStore;
  log: (ev: string, d?: any, level?: string)=>void;
  eventsPath: string;
  receiptsPath: string;
  keys?: { ed25519_priv_pem?: string };
};

function J(service: string) {
  return (ev: string, d:any={}, level="info") =>
    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ts:new Date().toISOString(),service,event:ev,level,...d})+"\n");
}

async function receipt(ctx: Ctx, span: any, decision="allow", note?: string) {
  const r = {
    ts: new Date().toISOString(),
    action: span.action,
    intent: span.intent,
    params: span.params || {},
    decision,
    note,
    policy_reasons: [], violations: [],
    hash: undefined, signature: undefined
  };
  await appendNdjsonLine(ctx.receiptsPath, r);
  return r;
}

function ensureDirs(paths: string[]) {
  for (const p of paths) mkdirSync(p, { recursive: true });
}

function sh(cmd: string, args: string[] = [], cwd?: string) {
  return new Promise<void>((res, rej) => {
    const child = spawn(cmd, args, { cwd, stdio: "inherit", env: process.env });
    child.on("exit", (code) => code === 0 ? res() : rej(new Error(`${cmd} ${args.join(" ")} => ${code}`)));
  });
}

function renderLaunchd(label: string, program: string[], logDir: string, runAtLoad=true, keepAlive=true) {
  return `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" \
"http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0"><dict>
  <key>Label</key><string>${label}</string>
  <key>ProgramArguments</key><array>
    ${program.map(s=>`<string>${s}</string>`).join("\n    ")}
  </array>
  <key>RunAtLoad</key><${runAtLoad}/>
  <key>KeepAlive</key><${keepAlive}/>
  <key>StandardOutPath</key><string>${join(logDir, label)}.out.log</string>
  <key>StandardErrorPath</key><string>${join(logDir, label)}.err.log</string>
</dict></plist>`;
}

async function installDirs(ctx: Ctx) {
  const { paths } = ctx.cfg;
  ensureDirs([paths.codeDir, paths.etcDir, paths.dataDir, paths.logDir, paths.stateDir, paths.outDir, paths.archiveDir]);
  await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.prepare.fs", 10);
}

function writeEnv(ctx: Ctx, p: {
  api_port:number; sse_port:number; pwa_port:number; cors:string[]; hmac_secret:string;
}) {
  const etc = ctx.cfg.paths.etcDir;
  const body = [
    `LAB256_INSTALLED=1`,
    `LAB256_CODE_DIR=${ctx.cfg.paths.codeDir}`,
    `LAB256_ETC_DIR=${ctx.cfg.paths.etcDir}`,
    `LAB256_DATA_DIR=${ctx.cfg.paths.dataDir}`,
    `LAB256_LOG_DIR=${ctx.cfg.paths.logDir}`,
    `LAB256_API_PORT=${p.api_port}`,
    `LAB256_SSE_PORT=${p.sse_port}`,
    `LAB256_MAX_MD_MB=2`,
    `LAB_MAX_INGESTS=50`,
    `LAB_CORS_ORIGINS=${p.cors.join(",")}`,
    `LAB_HMAC_SECRET=${p.hmac_secret}`,
    `LOGLEVEL=info`,
    `LOG_JSON=1`
  ].join("\n")+"\n";
  ensureDirs([etc]);
  writeFileSync(join(etc,"lab256.env"), body, "utf8");
}

function copyCode(src: string, dest: string) {
  // dev: plain copy of working dir/subdir into /opt tree
  ensureDirs([dest]);
  cpSync(resolve(src), dest, { recursive: true });
}

async function installLaunchd(ctx: Ctx, params: { api_port:number; sse_port:number; pwa_port:number }) {
  const labelApi = "com.voulezvous.lab256.api";
  const labelSse = "com.voulezvous.lab256.sse";
  const labelPwa = "com.voulezvous.lab256.pwa";

  const plistDir = "/Library/LaunchDaemons";
  const logDir = ctx.cfg.log.dir;
  ensureDirs([logDir]);

  const apiCmd = [process.execPath, resolve("bin/run_api.sh")];
  const sseCmd = [process.execPath, resolve("bin/run_sse.sh")];
  const pwaCmd = [process.execPath, resolve("bin/run_pwa.sh")];

  writeFileSync(join(plistDir, `${labelApi}.plist`), renderLaunchd(labelApi, apiCmd, logDir), "utf8");
  writeFileSync(join(plistDir, `${labelSse}.plist`), renderLaunchd(labelSse, sseCmd, logDir), "utf8");
  writeFileSync(join(plistDir, `${labelPwa}.plist`), renderLaunchd(labelPwa, pwaCmd, logDir), "utf8");

  await sh("launchctl", ["load", "-w", join(plistDir, `${labelApi}.plist`)]);
  await sh("launchctl", ["load", "-w", join(plistDir, `${labelSse}.plist`)]);
  await sh("launchctl", ["load", "-w", join(plistDir, `${labelPwa}.plist`)]);
}

async function verifyHealth(apiPort: number, ssePort: number) {
  // very small inline probes using curl if present
  try { await sh("curl", ["-fsS", `http://127.0.0.1:${apiPort}/healthz`]); } catch { throw new Error("API /healthz failed"); }
  try { await sh("curl", ["-fsS", `http://127.0.0.1:${ssePort}/events?topic=all`, "--max-time","1"]); } catch { /* SSE probe is best-effort */ }
}

export default {
  // plan-only: returns the trajectory (spans) first
  async handle_intent({ action, params }: any, rawCtx: any) {
    const cfg = loadConfig("installer-agent");
    const ctx: Ctx = {
      cfg,
      state: new StateStore(cfg.paths.stateDir),
      log: J("installer-agent"),
      eventsPath: join(cfg.paths.stateDir, "events.ndjson"),
      receiptsPath: join(cfg.paths.stateDir, "receipts.ndjson"),
      keys: rawCtx?.keys
    };

    const role = params?.role || "lab256-primary";

    const plan = [
      { action: "install.verify.host", intent: action, params },
      { action: "install.prepare.fs", intent: action, params },
      { action: "install.logline.core", intent: action, params },
      { action: "install.logline.extras", intent: action, params },
      { action: role === "lab8-worker" ? "install.lab8.worker" : "install.lab256", intent: action, params },
      { action: "install.pwa", intent: action, params },
      { action: "install.services.enable", intent: action, params },
      { action: "install.verify", intent: action, params }
    ];

    return { plan };
  },

  // executes each span; produces receipts & state updates
  async execute(span: any, rawCtx: any) {
    const cfg = loadConfig("installer-agent");
    const ctx: Ctx = {
      cfg,
      state: new StateStore(cfg.paths.stateDir),
      log: J("installer-agent"),
      eventsPath: join(cfg.paths.stateDir, "events.ndjson"),
      receiptsPath: join(cfg.paths.stateDir, "receipts.ndjson"),
      keys: rawCtx?.keys
    };

    const p = span.params || {};
    const api_port = p.api_port ?? 8090;
    const sse_port = p.sse_port ?? 8091;
    const pwa_port = p.pwa_port ?? 8088;
    const cors = p.origin_allow ?? ["http://localhost:"+pwa_port];
    const hmac_secret = p.hmac_secret ?? "";

    try {
      switch (span.action) {
        case "install.verify.host": {
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("verify.host", 5);
          // Check Bun, Node, curl
          await sh("bun", ["--version"]);
          await sh("node", ["-v"]);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", "host verified");
          break;
        }
        case "install.prepare.fs": {
          await installDirs(ctx);
          writeEnv(ctx, { api_port, sse_port, pwa_port, cors, hmac_secret });
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", "FHS prepared and env written");
          break;
        }
        case "install.logline.core": {
          const src = p.code_sources?.loglineos_dir || "./apps/loglineos";
          const dest = join(ctx.cfg.paths.codeDir, "loglineos");
          copyCode(src, dest);
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.logline.core", 25);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", `Copied ${src} -> ${dest}`);
          break;
        }
        case "install.logline.extras": {
          // Placeholder: copy extras, policies, etc
          const dest = join(ctx.cfg.paths.codeDir, "extras");
          ensureDirs([dest]);
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.logline.extras", 35);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", "extras prepared");
          break;
        }
        case "install.lab256": {
          const src = p.code_sources?.lab256_dir || "./apps/lab-256";
          const dest = join(ctx.cfg.paths.codeDir, "lab256");
          copyCode(src, dest);
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.lab256", 50);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", `Copied ${src} -> ${dest}`);
          break;
        }
        case "install.lab8.worker": {
          const src = p.code_sources?.lab256_dir || "./apps/lab-256";
          const dest = join(ctx.cfg.paths.codeDir, "lab8-worker");
          copyCode(src, dest);
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.lab8.worker", 50);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", `Copied ${src} -> ${dest} (worker profile)`);
          break;
        }
        case "install.pwa": {
          const src = p.code_sources?.pwa_dist || "./apps/lab-256/ui-pwa/dist";
          const dest = join(ctx.cfg.paths.codeDir, "pwa", "dist");
          copyCode(src, dest);
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.pwa", 65, { pwa_port });
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", `PWA dist copied ${src} -> ${dest}`);
          break;
        }
        case "install.services.enable": {
          await installLaunchd(ctx, { api_port, sse_port, pwa_port });
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.services.enable", 85);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", "launchd services loaded");
          break;
        }
        case "install.verify": {
          await verifyHealth(api_port, sse_port);
          await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.verify", 100);
          await receipt(ctx, span, "allow", "health checks passed");
          break;
        }
        default:
          await receipt(ctx, span, "deny", "unknown action");
      }
      return { ok: true, action: span.action };
    } catch (err: any) {
      await ctx.state.updatePhase("install.error", 0, { last_error: String(err?.message || err) });
      await appendNdjsonLine(ctx.eventsPath, { ts: new Date().toISOString(), type:"error", phase:"install", msg:String(err?.message||err) });
      await receipt(ctx, span, "deny", String(err?.message||err));
      return { ok: false, action: span.action, error: String(err?.message||err) };
    }
  }
};
```

* * *

4) Wire the agent into the runtime
==================================

```ts
// apps/runtime/agents/registry.ts
import Installer from "../../agents/installer/index.ts";
// ... other imports
export const registry = {
  "agent://installer.v1": Installer,
  // "agent://hk.v1": HK,
  // ...
};
```

HTTP entry (already present in your API—ensure it passes `agent_id,intent,params` to registry):

```ts
// apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts (excerpt)
router.post("/intent", async (req, res) => {
  const { agent_id, intent, params } = await readJsonBody(req);
  const agent = registry[agent_id];
  const plan = await agent.handle_intent({ action:intent, params }, {});
  // Execute trajectory in-process:
  for (const span of plan.plan) {
    const r = await agent.execute(span, {});
    if (!r.ok) return res.writeHead(500, {"Content-Type":"application/json"}).end(JSON.stringify(r));
  }
  res.writeHead(200, {"Content-Type":"application/json"}).end(JSON.stringify({ok:true, plan:plan.plan.length}));
});
```

* * *

5) launchd helper scripts (already compatible with PR-1..6)
===========================================================

```bash
# bin/run_api.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ETC_DIR="/etc/voulezvous/lab256"
[ -f "$ETC_DIR/lab256.env" ] && . "$ETC_DIR/lab256.env"
export LAB_API_PORT="${LAB256_API_PORT:-${LAB_API_PORT:-8090}}"
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"
exec bun run apps/lab-256/api/src/server.ts
```

```bash
# bin/run_sse.sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ETC_DIR="/etc/voulezvous/lab256"
[ -f "$ETC_DIR/lab256.env" ] && . "$ETC_DIR/lab256.env"
export LAB_SSE_PORT="${LAB256_SSE_PORT:-${LAB_SSE_PORT:-8091}}"
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"
exec bun run apps/lab-256/sse/src/server.ts
```

```bash
# bin/run_pwa.sh — serve static dist via Bun
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ETC_DIR="/etc/voulezvous/lab256"
[ -f "$ETC_DIR/lab256.env" ] && . "$ETC_DIR/lab256.env"
PORT="${LAB256_PWA_PORT:-${LAB_PWA_PORT:-8088}}"
DIST="${LAB256_CODE_DIR:-$(pwd)}/pwa/dist"
exec bun run --eval "Bun.serve({port:${PORT},fetch:r=>new Response(Bun.file('${DIST}'+(new URL(r.url).pathname||'/index.html')))})"
```

* * *

6) What it installs (the “five”)
================================

*   **LogLineOS Core** → `/opt/voulezvous/loglineos/current`
*   **LogLineOS Extras** → `/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current/extras` (policies, tools)
*   **Lab 256 (primary)** or **Lab 8GB (worker)** → `/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current` (or `lab8-worker`)
*   **PWA** (static dist) → `/opt/voulezvous/lab256/current/pwa/dist`
*   **Services** (`launchd`) → API(8090), SSE(8091), PWA(8088)

Configs live in `/etc/voulezvous/lab256/lab256.env`  
Data/Receipts/Snapshots in `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/*`  
Logs in `/var/log/voulezvous/lab256/*`

* * *

7) Two-Mac setup (LAB-256 + LAB-8GB)
====================================

*   On **LAB-256 (primary)** run the `install.all` as shown (role=`lab256-primary`).
*   On **LAB-8GB (worker)** run the same intent with `role="lab8-worker"` and (optionally) `origin_allow` pointing to LAB-256 PWA origin. The agent uses the same FHS but places code under `lab8-worker` and skips PWA if wanted.

* * *

8) Acceptance checks (automated)
================================

After `install.verify`:

*   `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8090/healthz` → `{"ok":true,...}`
*   `curl -N http://127.0.0.1:8091/events?topic=cycles` → live events
*   `ls /var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/state/snapshots/` → snapshot JSON present
*   `open http://localhost:8088` → PWA dashboard

Receipts: `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/state/receipts.ndjson`  
Events: `/var/lib/voulezvous/lab256/state/events.ndjson`

* * *

That’s it
---------

You now have **one native Installer Agent** that plans → executes → verifies the **five installers** in a single trajectory, emits receipts, and keeps everything idempotent and auditable—fully aligned with SpLLM/LogLineOS.
